title: Remote Federal Contract Research & Proposal Specialist
description:
About Us



US Veterans Solutions is a U.S.-based, Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Business (SDVOSB) focused on securing and executing federal government contracts.



We are actively building a high-performance remote support team to assist with identifying, organizing, and preparing competitive proposals for federal opportunities.



This is an opportunity to work directly with leadership on real contract pursuits—not general VA work.



Position Overview



We are seeking a highly detail-oriented, reliable, and intelligent professional to support our federal contracting efforts on a project-by-project basis.



This is NOT a salaried role. Work is assigned based on active contract opportunities. Ideal for someone who prefers flexible, performance-based work and wants to grow within U.S. government contracting.



What You Will Be Doing



You will play a critical role in helping us identify and prepare for contract opportunities:



Research federal contract opportunities (primarily via SAM.gov and related platforms)

Review solicitations and extract key requirements

Organize documents and build structured proposal folders

Assist in drafting and formatting proposal sections (based on templates and guidance)

Assemble complete proposal packages for final executive review

Track deadlines, submission requirements, and compliance items

Who This Role Is For



This role is ideal for someone who:



Is highly organized and detail-driven

Can read and understand complex documents

Takes ownership and follows instructions precisely

Communicates clearly and professionally in English

Wants to build long-term experience in U.S. federal contracting

Is motivated by growth, performance, and opportunity

Preferred Experience (Strong Advantage)

Administrative support, research, or document management

Experience with procurement, bidding, or proposal preparation

Familiarity with U.S. government contracting platforms (SAM.gov, etc.)

Experience working with structured processes or compliance-heavy work

Bonus / Highly Preferred Skills



Candidates with the following skillsets will stand out:



Experience using AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, etc.) for research, drafting, and workflow efficiency

Strong writing and editing skills (professional, structured communication)

Experience with RFP/RFQ response development

Familiarity with Microsoft Word formatting (styles, tables, compliance formatting)

Experience with Excel or Google Sheets for tracking opportunities

CRM or project management tools (Trello, Asana, ClickUp, etc.)

Basic understanding of U.S. business, contracting, or compliance processes

Ability to analyze documents and summarize key requirements quickly

Experience supporting U.S.-based clients or companies

Growth Opportunity (IMPORTANT)



This role is designed to grow with the company.



For the right individual who demonstrates reliability, accuracy, and strong performance, there is a clear path toward:



Consistent, ongoing work

Increased responsibility and leadership in proposal development

The opportunity to transition into a full-time role



Target compensation of up to $20/hour at 40 hours per week may be attainable within 12 months for high-performing individuals who grow with the company and contribute to successful contract execution.



Work Structure

100% Remote

Project-based (no fixed monthly hours initially)

Flexible schedule, but deadlines are strict

Work volume will increase as contracts increase

Compensation

Paid per project/task (not hourly salary at start)

Rates based on complexity and experience

Performance-based growth opportunities

Potential for long-term, stable work as contract pipeline expands

Important Notes

This is a performance-based opportunity, not a traditional salaried position at the start

You will be working directly with a U.S.-based CEO

Company is transitioning branding from US Veterans Security to US Veterans Solutions

Application Instructions (IMPORTANT)



To be considered, you MUST follow these steps:



Submit your resume

Include a short paragraph explaining your experience with research, admin work, or proposals

Answer this question:

“How would you approach reviewing a contract document to identify key requirements?”

Include the word PRECISION at the top of your application



Applications that do not follow instructions will not be considered.



What We Are Building



We are building a long-term, high-performance remote team to support federal contract operations.



This is an opportunity to be part of something that is growing, structured, and results-driven.



We are not looking for average. We are looking for professionals who execute with precision and are ready to grow.

title: SEO EXECUTION VA (SearchAtlas-Based) – REAL ESTATE
description:
ROLE OVERVIEW

I'm hiring an SEO Execution Virtual Assistant, not a strategist and not an agency.

This role is for someone who already understands on-page SEO and page-level optimization and can execute tasks inside a defined system using SearchAtlas.

You will not be "learning SEO from scratch."

You will be trained on my workflows and tools, but you must already understand how SEO actually works.

This role supports a U.S.-based real estate business focused on local SEO and geographic market domination.



WHAT YOU WILL DO (NON-NEGOTIABLE)

• Perform keyword research inside SearchAtlas

• Map one keyword ? one page correctly

• Analyze SERPs and identify intent mismatches

• Optimize existing blog posts and landing pages

• Create SEO-optimized blog drafts

• Write meta titles and descriptions

• Implement internal links intentionally

• Apply page-level schema (basic)

• Monitor Google Search Console for indexing and issues

• Track rankings and maintain a simple SEO log

• Execute video-to-blog SEO workflows

• Explain your work during live screen-share reviews

If you don't know how to do any of the above without guessing, this role is not a fit.



REQUIRED EXPERIENCE (PLEASE READ CAREFULLY)

You must:

• Understand the difference between a keyword and a page

• Understand search intent (informational vs local vs transactional)

• Have optimized at least one real blog or page before

• Have used any SEO tool (Ahrefs, SEMrush, Surfer, etc.)

• Be comfortable working inside a CMS or website builder

• Be willing to do live screen-share audits

• Be able to explain why you made SEO changes

If you have never optimized a page that ranks, do not apply.



TOOLS YOU WILL USE

• SearchAtlas (training on this tool provided)

• Google Search Console

• Google Analytics (basic)

• GoHighLevel (GHL)

• Google Docs / Sheets



PAY & STRUCTURE

• 10–15 hours per week

• Long-term role

• Paid trial week (reduced rate)

• Weekly reviews and feedback

• Clear SOPs and checklists

Performance determines pay increases.



HOW TO APPLY (REQUIRED)

Your application must answer these questions:

1. What is the difference between a keyword and a page?

2. What does search intent mean?

3. What SEO tools have you used?

4. Have you optimized a blog post before? Briefly explain what you did.

5. Are you comfortable doing a live screen-share test?

Applications without answers will be ignored.



This role includes a live screen-share test. If that makes you uncomfortable, do not apply.

title: APS Consulting Recruitment Notice
description:
APS Consulting is a Sydney-based advisory firm providing comprehensive strategic consulting services to clients worldwide. As we continue to expand our global footprint, we are seeking experienced Overseas Consultants with strong expertise in government relations, and a deep understanding of political affairs, economics, law, and public policy.



Scope of Work and Responsibilities  

Consultants in this role primarily support research, policy analysis, and advisory work related to overseas political, regulatory, and governance developments. Typical tasks include:



Conducting background research and policy monitoring



Stakeholder mapping and engagement analysis



Drafting analytical briefs and memos



Supporting scenario planning and risk assessments for client decision-making



Types of Clients and Projects  

Our clients generally include private enterprises, professional institutions, and organizations with cross-border interests that require an informed understanding of overseas political, regulatory, or public-sector environments. Projects are research- and analysis-driven, and may relate to:



Public policy trends



Government processes



Geopolitical risk assessments



Work Structure (approximately 20 hours per week)  

This is a flexible, part-time consultancy arrangement. Work is typically project-based, with weekly hours varying depending on deliverables and timelines. Coordination is conducted remotely, and schedules are discussed in advance to align with both project requirements and consultant availability.



Preferred Backgrounds  

Candidates with prior experience in the following sectors will be given priority consideration:



Government or public administration



Law firms or legal advisory institutions



Media or strategic communications organizations



Think tanks or research institutions



Consulting or public relations firms



Non-governmental or international organizations



Compensation and Benefits  

We offer competitive compensation packages and flexible work arrangements tailored to global professionals.

title: SharePoint / Microsoft 365 Architect
description:
*KINDLY READ THE JOB DESCRIPTION & THE APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS CAREFULLY.*



About the Organization 



EJF Real Estate Services and The Nabo Group are growing organizations focused on real estate investment and property/HOA management across multiple regions. Our operations rely heavily on structured document management, secure collaboration, and scalable systems to support property portfolios, boards, and internal teams. 



We are undergoing a transformation to modernize and standardize our Microsoft 365 environment, with a strong focus on SharePoint Online, governance, and operational efficiency. 



Overview 



We are seeking an experienced SharePoint & Microsoft 365 Architect to design and implement a scalable, secure, and governance-driven collaboration environment tailored to a multi-entity real estate and property management organization within the Corporate IT Department. 



This role will focus on building a structured and standardized environment across SharePoint Online, Microsoft Teams, OneDrive, and Microsoft Entra ID, enabling efficient document management, controlled access, and future-ready AI and automation capabilities. 



The ideal candidate is a strategic architect, not just an administrator, with experience designing environments that support distributed teams, regional operations, and complex permission models.



Duties & Responsibilities:



Architecture & Design:

 

•  Design enterprise-grade SharePoint Online architecture aligned to:  

 -  Properties / communities (HOAs)  

 -  Regions / portfolios  

 -  Internal departments (Finance, Operations, Legal, etc.)  

•  Implement a hub-and-spoke model with clear hierarchy and navigation  

•  Define scalable information architecture:  

 -  Metadata vs folder structures  

 -  Content types and taxonomy  

 -  Standardized document libraries  

•  Establish site provisioning models and templates for repeatable property onboarding  

•  Align SharePoint with Microsoft Teams and OneDrive usage patterns  



Governance & Compliance:

 

•  Develop and document a SharePoint governance framework tailored to property management operations:  

 -  Naming conventions (properties, regions, departments)  

 -  Site lifecycle management (onboarding/offboarding properties)  

 -  Permission models (board members, internal staff, vendors)  

•  Implement data governance and compliance controls:  

 -  Retention policies for property records and financial documents  

 -  Sensitivity labels and secure document handling  

 -  External sharing controls for board members and third parties  

•  Ensure alignment with real estate and property management operational needs  



Microsoft 365 Integration:  



•  Architect seamless integration across:  

 -  SharePoint Online  

 -  Microsoft Teams (for property and internal collaboration)  

 -  OneDrive (personal vs organizational data boundaries)  

 -  Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) 

•  Design scalable identity and access strategies:  

 -  Role-based access (internal staff, board members, vendors)  

 -  Group-based provisioning  

 -  Conditional access awareness  



Automation & AI Readiness:

 

•  Structure SharePoint for Microsoft Copilot readiness (clean, well-organized data)  

•  Identify opportunities for:  

 -  Power Automate workflows (approvals, document routing, onboarding)  

 -  Process automation for property management operations  

•  Improve search, discoverability, and document intelligence  



Migration & Optimization:



•  Assess current SharePoint and file storage environments  

•  Identify gaps, duplication, and inefficiencies  

•  Develop a migration and consolidation strategy  

•  Optimize usability for both:  

 -  Internal teams  

 -  Non-technical users (property managers, board members)  



Documentation & Knowledge Transfer:

 

•  Deliver:  

 -  Architecture diagrams  

 -  Governance framework documentation  

 -  Implementation and rollout roadmap  

•  Provide knowledge transfer to internal IT leadership and support teams  





Required Qualifications:

 

•  5+ years of experience with SharePoint Online (modern experience required)  

•  5+ years across the Microsoft 365 ecosystem  

•  Proven experience designing enterprise or multi-entity SharePoint architectures  

•  Strong understanding of:  

 -  Microsoft Teams & SharePoint integration  

 -  Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD)  

 -  Complex permission and access control models  

•  Experience with:  

 -  Metadata and taxonomy design  

 -  Governance frameworks  

 -  Scalable site provisioning strategies  





Preferred Qualifications:

 

•  Experience supporting property management, real estate, or HOA environments  

•  Experience with multi-entity or portfolio-based organizations  

•  Familiarity with:  

 -  Microsoft Purview (retention, compliance, labeling)  

 -  Power Platform (Power Automate, Power Apps)  

 -  Microsoft Copilot readiness and data structuring  

• Microsoft certifications (nice to have):  

 -  MS-100 / MS-101  

 -  MS-700  

 -  SharePoint / M365 certifications 



Working Hours: 9:00 am – 5:00 pm EST; Monday – Friday (US time zones)



Benefits: HMO and PTO after 90 days.



How to apply:

1. Please check out the company website before submitting an application: 
.

2. Send an
 attachment with your updated resume in PDF format.

3. Please indicate that you're hourly rate in USD..

4. Please send a link to your video recording introducing yourself & your relevant experience based on the job description, and include your OnlineJobs.PH profile link.



Kindly send your application to 
 the subject line, kindly indicate the position SharePoint / Microsoft 365 Architect - your name.



*Applicants who will apply through onlinejobs.ph will not be considered.

*Shortlisted applicants will be contacted by
 for interview scheduling.

Looking forward to working with you!

title: Video Editor
description:
We're hiring a video editor for a growing 7-figure DTC health and wellness brand running paid ads on Meta.



This is a performance marketing role, not a creative agency role. You'll be editing direct-response video ads — short-form (45s-2min) to start, with the opportunity to grow into longer VSL-style content (2-5min) as we scale together.



What you'll be doing:



• Editing short-form direct-response video ads from storyboarded briefs

• Turning around 1-2 minute ads within 24 hours

• Working with UGC footage, stock clips, and AI-generated content

• Iterating fast — new hooks, new leads, chunking winners into different lengths

• Following a clear SOP for when and how to iterate on winning ads



What we need from you:



• Experience editing direct-response or performance marketing video ads (not brand/corporate)

• Experience editing for ecommerce brands selling physical products (not info)

• Proficiency in Premiere Pro

• You can deliver a 1-minute ad edit in under 24 hours, consistently

• You understand pacing, text overlays, pattern interrupts, and engagement-driven editing

• Experience with AI and creating custom Broll and UGC

• Bonus: you've edited VSLs before



How this works:



1. Apply with your portfolio (must include direct-response ad examples)

2. If we like what we see, you'll get a paid trial task — a 1-minute ad with a 24-hour deadline

3. If this goes well, we move into a Trial period. This is project-based for ~1 month. If speed and quality are consistent, this moves to a full-time role at $2,000/mo + performance bonuses, and potential to grow within the company, and earn even more.



We are scaling fast and need someone who can keep up. Speed is the #1 thing we're hiring for. If you can't turn around a 1-minute edit in a day, this isn't the right fit.



Open to editors based in Latin America, Philippines, India, Pakistan, and broader Asia.



To apply: Send your portfolio and a short note on your experience with direct-response video ads (The brands you have worked for). Include your typical turnaround time for a 1-minute edit, and the word blue.

title: Full-Time Video Editing & Operations Assistant (Comedy Ads)
description:
I’m looking for ONE full-time VA to help edit short videos that run as ads for live comedy shows.



This is a long-term role.



You’ll start with simple, repetitive video editing work. As you prove accuracy and consistency, you’ll be trained to take on additional operational tasks including basic Meta ad setup, reporting, and system support.



What This Role Is



This role is for people who take pride in precision and consistency - not creativity.



Most of the work is structured, repeatable, and process-driven.



You will be following clear instructions and producing high volumes of accurate work.



Starting Tasks (Day 1)



• Create simple outro posters using Canva templates

• Add provided outros (with music) to stand-up clips

• Edit short 30–60 sec videos using predefined rules (not creative editing)

• Censor clips by muting swear words and covering captions (if required)

• Export in correct ad-ready formats

• Upload and organize files correctly



Expanded Responsibilities (After Training)



Once you’ve proven accuracy and consistency, you’ll take on additional operational tasks such as:



• Setting up ad campaigns using templates (Meta Ads Manager)

• Updating campaign data in Google Sheets / ClickUp

• Keeping records and delivery systems organized and up to date

• Assisting with repeatable admin and workflow tasks



These tasks are structured and system-driven - not open-ended admin work.



Video production remains your primary responsibility. Additional tasks are introduced only after output standards are consistently met.



What Good Looks Like (First 30 Days)



• You can produce 20–40 clips per day with near-zero errors

• You follow instructions exactly without improvising

• You rarely need corrections on formatting, naming, or censoring

• You flag unclear instructions instead of guessing

• You work quickly while maintaining accuracy



What You Need



• Reliable internet and a laptop

• Basic video editing experience (CapCut, Premiere, DaVinci, etc.)

• Canva or similar tool experience

• Strong attention to detail

• Fluent written English

• Full-time availability (40 hrs/week)

• Able to join occasional calls (especially during training)

• Coachable, consistent, and responsive



Who This Is For



• You want stable, long-term work

• You prefer following systems over guessing

• You double-check details like dates, locations, and file names

• You take ownership of mistakes and fix them quickly

• You care more about doing the job right than doing it “your way”

• You’re comfortable learning new tools when shown how



Important Notes



• This is a high-volume role — speed and accuracy both matter

• The content includes strong language and adult humour (stand-up comedy)

• Mistakes are expected early, but repeated mistakes on the same instructions are not acceptable

• If accuracy is consistently poor, the role will not continue

• If something is unclear, you are expected to stop and ask — guessing is not acceptable



This Is NOT



• A creative role

• A strategy role

• A media buying role

• A part-time position



Quick Summary



This role is mostly mechanical execution.



You will be:



Creating Canva-based outro posters

Attaching them to clips

Following strict rules around formats, naming, cities, and dates



There is:



No creative decision-making

No clip selection

No “finding the funny”



Accuracy, consistency, and reliability matter more than creativity.



To Be Considered



• Start your application with the word COMEDY

• Include 6 AP Points in your application



Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be reviewed.



Final Note



Most applicants will not be a fit for this role.



Attention to detail is tested heavily.



If you are reliable, precise, and coachable, you’ll get long-term work and increasing responsibility over time.

title: Be Our YouTube Editor
description:
— WHO WE ARE —…



We are a small group of friends looking to make our mark on the YouTube landscape, with our action-packed and humorous gameplay and challenges.



Check out our channel: 



— WHAT WE‘RE LOOKING FOR —



We’re looking for remote, highly experienced Video Editors who aspire to be the best YouTube editors in the world. Period.



If hired, you will spend the majority of your time editing and animating fast-paced, highly engaging YouTube videos (our style could be compared to Game Theory, MortarMarv, or Finzar).



Our team culture is always supportive and encouraging, but you should also expect to be held to an exceptionally high standard because that’s how we become the best in the world at what we do (If you hate getting lots of revisions/feedback on your work, you won’t fit in here).



Your day-to-day will also involve analyzing previous videos' Viewer Retention Analytics Graphs to uncover opportunities to improve future edits.



— THE IDEAL CANDIDATE —



Here are the main skills and abilities we’re looking for in our applicants:



VIDEO EDITING (Required): Obviously, you must be an exceptionally talented video editor from a technical perspective. However, you must also have the passion, creativity, and sense of humor to be able to turn bland, average footage into highly engaging videos that educate AND entertain viewers.



ANIMATION (Required): You must be capable of creating high-quality 2d overlay, titling, and keyframing animations that visually illustrate the concepts we discuss in our videos.



— YOUR KPIs —



Your performance will be graded using the following KPIs:



1 Good Audience Retention, which we’ll be able to measure and learn from through our YouTube analytics.



2 The number of revisions the videos you edit require before being finalised (the less, the better)



3 The number of hours it takes you to create a video (the less, the better)



— WHAT'S IN IT FOR YOU —



Here are just some of the areas where you’ll have the opportunity to learn and grow at our channel.



STABLE & SECURE: This is a very stable and secure job. Our most senior editors have been working with us full-time for 3+ years. No more wishy-washy freelance gigs.



LEADERSHIP OPPORTUNITIES: If you prove yourself and we continue with our current growth rate, you will likely be presented with senior opportunities down the track.



LEARN FROM THE BEST IN THE WORLD: With us, you get to learn from people who are constantly striving to be the best in the world. We’re supportive and encouraging of one another, but we also hold each other to very high standards.



CREATIVE FREEDOM: Once you’ve mastered our style, we encourage outside-the-box thinking. We’re always open to experimenting with new techniques and styles, and then we analyze the data to see how viewers react.



FLEXIBLE WORK HOURS: You can work whenever you want within reason; however, we expect you to be available for tea
etings (We are based in California, and tea
etings are usually in the late evening PT).



— TO APPLY —



If you're interested, please submit the following form (No need for a CV or cover letter. It’s not the 1900s anymore :D):






Please note: All successful job applications will start with a virtual interview and trial project/s to make sure that we’re the right fit for each other.



Thank you for your consideration, and best of luck on your job search!

title: Real Estate Appointment Setter / Caller (Long-Term)
description:
READ CAREFULLY BEFORE APPLYING



We are a fast-growing Canadian real estate team looking for a professional appointment setter to call our inbound and outbound leads. This is NOT a casual VA role. This is a results-driven calling position.

If you are confident on the phone, disciplined, and comfortable speaking with buyers and sellers, we want to talk to you.



>> What You Will Do

Call real estate leads daily (buyers & sellers)

Answer
s and text messages from buyers and sellers

Qualify prospects using a clear script

Book appointments for our agents

Log every call, note, and outcome in our CRM

Follow a strict call & follow-up system

No cold freelancing. No guessing. No skipping leads.



>> Requirements (Non-Negotiable)

Clear, confident English (neutral accent preferred)

1+ year experience in calling / appointment setting / sales

Comfortable calling North America (Canada / U.S.)

Able to handle objections calmly and professionally

Reliable internet, headset, and quiet work environment

Organized and disciplined with CRM updates

Real estate experience is a big plus, but strong callers can be trained.



>> Work Schedule

Part-time (20 hr/week)

Must be available during pacific time from 2-7 pm pacific time Tuesday, Thursday, Saturday, Sunday

Consistent schedule required



>> Pay Structure

Base pay: $5-7 CAD/hour (based on experience)

Bonuses per qualified appointment booked

Performance-based raises for strong results

Good callers earn more. Period.



>> What We Offer

Long-term, stable work (not a short gig)

Clear scripts and systems

Growth opportunity into senior roles





>> This Job Is NOT For You If:

You are afraid of calling

You need constant reminders to work

You skip CRM updates

You disappear or make excuses

We move fast and expect professionalism.



>> HOW TO APPLY (IMPORTANT)



To apply, you MUST include:

A short introduction (2–3 paragraphs)

Your calling or sales experience

Your internet speed and headset

Your availability (hours per day & timezone)

The word “APPOINTMENT” in your subject line

Applications that ignore instructions will be rejected automatically.



>> If you are confident, disciplined, and hungry to perform — apply now.

title: Document Editor: 2-3 day project
description:
I'm looking for a detail-oriented document editor to implement a prescriptive revision guide across four (4) long-form fiction manuscripts.



The work:

- You'll receive the completed manuscripts and a compiled editorial guide containing all required find-and-replace edits. 

- Each edit is formatted as SEARCH FOR / REPLACE WITH with the exact text strings provided. 

- Your job is to locate each string in the manuscript and make the substitution accurately. 

- This is implementation work, not creative editing. 

- You won't be rewriting or making judgement calls. 

- The guides tell you exactly what to change and what to leave alone.



What I need from you:

- Expert working in Google Docs with long documents. 

- Strong attention to detail. 

- The ability to work methodically through a sequential list without skipping or reordering. 

- A willingness to sign a strict non-disclosure agreement before receiving any materials. 



This is an unpublished creative work and confidentiality is non-negotiable.



Timeline and budget:

- This is a 2 to 3 day project. 

- Please include your day rate or project rate when you apply.



To apply:

Send a brief message about your experience with document editing or proofreading, particularly with long-form or fiction manuscripts. 



Let me know your availability and rate.

title: High-Performance Executive Operator (Data & Operations)
description:
This is not a traditional Executive Assistant role.



We are looking for a high-performance operator who can combine data intelligence with operational execution and work directly alongside a fast-moving, visionary leader.



This role is for someone who can bring structure to data, clarity to decisions, and execution to leadership initiatives.



If you thrive in fast environments, think analytically, and operate with ownership, keep reading.



Who We Are:



DAP Brands is a community of high performers building businesses that create real impact.



Our leadership team moves quickly, thinks strategically, and expects people to take responsibility rather than wait for instructions.



We value:

• Speed

• Accountability

• Emotional maturity

• Precision

• Ownership

• Loyalty to the mission (not ego)



What This Role Really Is:



This role combines Data Analysis + Executive Operations.



Your first responsibility is to bring order to data across the organization: Cleaning, structuring, and translating it into clear insights.



Once the data foundation is organized, you will operate as a high-level executive, helping leadership execute faster and smarter.



You will help:



• Clean, organize, and analyze business data

• Turn raw data into meaningful reports and insights

• Support leadership with decision-ready information

• Protect your leader’s time and priorities

• Enforce accountability across leadership initiatives

• Translate strategy into structured execution

• Identify problems before they become fires

• Move initiatives forward without constant direction

• Bring clarity to complex situations



You will sit close to leadership.

You will see how decisions are made.

You must handle that with professionalism, discretion, and maturity.



What This Role Is NOT



This role is not for someone who:



• Needs constant praise or reassurance

• Struggles with direct feedback

• Prefers slow corporate environments

• Gets overwhelmed by changing priorities

• Waits for instructions instead of solving problems

• Is uncomfortable working with data and systems

• Frequently job hops every year



We operate in a high-accountability environment.



If you need tone cushioning or excessive structure, this will likely feel uncomfortable.



What Success Looks Like (First 90 Days):



Within your first 90 days, you:



• Organize and clean key business data sets

• Create structured reporting for leadership decisions

• Build dashboards or reports that bring visibility to operations

• Run leadership meetings with clear structure and follow-through

• Anticipate your leader’s needs before they are spoken

• Track priorities and ensure accountability across teams

• Bring solutions instead of just raising issues

• Operate calmly and clearly under pressure



The Profile That Thrives Here



You likely:



• Have experience in data analysis, reporting, or business analytics

• Are comfortable working with spreadsheets, dashboards, and structured data

• Have supported a founder or senior executive before

• Have stayed 3+ years in at least one meaningful role

• Enjoy solving complex operational problems

• Can build systems and workflows from scratch

• Communicate clearly and directly

• Are emotionally regulated and professional under pressure

• Think in outcomes and insights, not just tasks



You are someone who:



• Notices patterns others miss

• Brings clarity where others see chaos

• Moves things forward without being asked twice



Compensation & Growth:



This role offers:



• Competitive compensation aligned with performance

• Direct exposure to executive decision-making

• The opportunity to shape internal systems and operations

• Long-term growth as the company scales



At DAP Brands, we reward impact and ownership, not just tenure.



Final Note



This role is for someone who wants to build, solve, and operate at a high level.



If you enjoy working with data, solving operational problems, and supporting leadership execution, this could be an excellent fit.



If you're looking for a comfortable, slow-paced corporate role, this probably isn't it.



But if you want to operate at a high level and help build something meaningful, apply on our website 
/careers and write MAVERICK before your name.

title: Integrations Associate – Automation & Systems
description:
DAP Brands is seeking a detail-oriented, technically savvy, and solution-driven Integrations Associate to support our mission of building seamless, scalable automations across the organization. This role goes beyond task execution. You’ll work closely with our Technology Integration Manager to design, maintain, and optimize workflows that improve efficiency across Sales, Marketing, and Operations.



If you enjoy connecting systems, solving process gaps, and leveraging automation tools to create reliable, intelligent workflows, this role is for you.



Key Responsibilities:

- Integration Setup & Maintenance: Assist in setting up, testing, launching, and maintaining integrations using platforms such as Zapier, Make, and GoHighLevel. Monitor automations for accuracy and performance, troubleshoot issues, and implement long-term fixes.

- Automation & Workflow Optimization: Identify inefficiencies in existing processes and propose smart automation solutions. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand needs and build tailored workflows.

- Technical Support: Work with APIs, webhooks, and basic scripting (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, SQL) to resolve data sync issues, system errors, and performance bottlenecks.

- Documentation & Training: Document workflows, integrations, and processes to ensure scalability. Assist in training internal users on newly built systems and automation tools.



Tools You’ll Work With:

- Automation & Integrations: Zapier, Make, Webhooks

- CRMs & Operations Tools: GoHighLevel, ClickFunnels, Stripe, ClickUp

- Collaboration Tools: Google Sheets, Zoom, Google Workspace

- Scripting & APIs: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, SQL



Application Instructions:

Please submit your application through the online form provided: 



- To be considered, you MUST type “MAVERICK” in front of your full name if you are applying via our website: 

- If you are an Integrations Associate who takes ownership, thinks in systems, and thrives in a fast-moving, automation-driven environment, we invite you to apply.

title: Home Care - Caregiver/Healthcare Recruiter
description:
About Us



At Helper Heroes, we’re on a mission to provide exceptional care and support to home care agencies in America. We believe in creating a true partnership where our employees act and feel like a true member of the American team they are matched with. Our goal is to help them create a compassionate, innovative, and efficient care experience for their clients and their families. To achieve this, we’re looking for a Helper Hero to join our team and help us shine even brighter!



About You



What sets us apart is YOU. You’re not just any virtual tea
mber—you’re a Helper Hero! If you’re passionate about making a difference, have a knack for organization, and thrive in a fast-paced, mission-driven environment, you’re who we are looking for. If you’re the kind of person who loves tackling behind-the-scenes tasks with a smile, ensuring that our clients' caregivers and clients receive the best possible support, apply today!



Job Overview



We are seeking a proactive, detail-oriented Recruiter to support recruitment, caregiver

onboarding, and administrative tasks for a growing home care business. This role is

ideal for someone with experience in healthcare recruitment and a passion for

streamlining operations. You will work closely with the owner to alleviate their workload,

enabling them to focus on business growth and client care.



Key Responsibilities



1. Recruitment & Onboarding:



- Manage end-to-end caregiver recruitment: screen applicants, schedule interviews, and coordinate orientation/training.

- Process onboarding documentation via ADP Workforce (e.g., electronic I-9s, employee handbook distribution).

- Track and ensure completion of state-mandated caregiver training (e.g.,via Active Insights).



2. Administrative Support



- Answer inbound calls during peak hours and manage voi
s/lead inquiries.

- Assist with client intake calls and referral source coordination.

- Provide general administrative support (e.g., scheduling, data entry).



3. Systems & Collaboration



- Utilize platforms like WellSky or Axiscare (training provided) to streamline workflows.

- Collaborate with the owner to refine processes and maintain organization.



Qualifications:



- Excellent written/verbal communication (fluent English).

- Strong organizational and time-management abilities.

- Proficiency with CRM systems,
 management, and cloud-based tools (e.g., Google Drive).

- Proactive mindset—anticipate needs before they arise.

- Proven experience in recruitment, specifically in healthcare or home care staffing.

- Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to build rapport with candidates and tea
mbers.

- Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail.

- Ability to work independently and manage time effectively in a fast-paced environment.

- Familiarity with recruitment platforms and CRM tools

- A positive attitude, sense of humor, and a passion for helping others.



Attributes of a Successful Helper Hero:



- Empathy: You genuinely care about people and want to make a difference in their lives.

- Organization: You’re a master of multitasking and can keep everything running smoothly.

- Communication: You speak impeccable English. You’re a clear and friendly communicator, both written and verbal.

- Problem Solver: You can see the big picture when resolving complex issues and follow through until you reach full resolution.

- Self Starter: You work effectively without supervision and follow processes precisely.

- Reliability: You’re dependable, punctual, and always ready to step up when needed.



Why You’ll Love Working with Us:



- Competitive Base Pay: $6.50/hr for the first 6 months. (Among the highest in the homecare industry)

- Make a Difference: You’ll play a key role in ensuring that clients receive the best

care possible.

- Growth Opportunities: As we grow, so will you! There’s plenty of room for

advancement and learning.

- Fun & Supportive Team: We’re a tight-knit group that values collaboration,

compassion, and a good laugh.



Ready to Be a Hero?



If you’re excited about the opportunity to join our team, where you’ll be appreciated,

have a sense of purpose and inclusion and are willing to help us create a brighter, more

compassionate care experience, we can’t wait to hear from you! Apply today and let’s

make a difference together.



IMPORTANT: Directly apply and send your resume on our website: 
/helperheroes/

title: Podcast Operations Manager
description:
We are a growing US-based podcast production agency looking for a Podcast Operations Manager to help oversee production, keep projects moving, maintain quality, and support a fast-paced agency environment.



This is a long-term role for someone who wants to grow, take on more responsibility, and move into higher leadership over time. You must be able to work EST hours.



What You’ll Be Responsible For

• Overseeing podcast production workflows across multiple client accounts

• Coordinating production timelines and making sure projects stay on track

• Making sure tea
mbers are moving on time and following through on deliverables

• Communicating with clients as needed to keep production clear and organized

• Reviewing audio, video, show notes, titles, thumbnails, and final deliverables for quality and accuracy

• Catching issues early before they become deadline or client problems

• Jumping in on production tasks when needed to make sure deadlines are met

• Supporting publishing schedules and helping content go out on time

• Assisting with planning, tracking, and overall production organization

• Training, mentoring, and giving feedback to editors and show notes writers

• Helping maintain and improve quality standards across the team

• Improving workflows, systems, SOPs, and internal processes where needed



You Must Have

• Previous agency experience, ideally in podcasting, content marketing, media, or client services

• Strong experience with podcast production workflows

• Experience managing multiple clients, deadlines, and moving parts at once

• Strong project management and organizational ability

• Experience reviewing work and maintaining quality standards

• Strong writing judgment for show notes, titles, and content packaging

• Experience editing podcast audio and video

• Experience training, mentoring, or improving the work of editors and writers

• Clear and proactive communication

• Strong ownership and accountability

• Ability to work ahead and not leave important work until the last minute



Bonus Points If You Can

• Improve workflows and make the agency more efficient

• Build or refine SOPs and internal systems

• Strengthen accountability and follow-through across the team

• Spot bottlenecks and solve problems before they affect delivery



This Role Is Best For Someone Who

• Takes ownership instead of needing constant reminders

• Naturally keeps projects organized and moving

• Has high standards and genuinely cares about quality

• Is dependable and follows through

• Can manage deadlines in a fast-paced environment

• Can give direct, helpful feedback that improves the team’s work

• Wants long-term growth and more responsibility over time



How to Apply

• Your CV / resume

• Your portfolio

• Your website, if you have one

• A short message explaining your agency experience

• Examples of podcasts or content you have worked on

• The tools you use for audio editing, video editing, project management, and content repurposing

• A short note on any experience you have training, mentoring, or improving the work of tea
mbers

• A short note on any workflows, systems, or processes you have helped improve

• Include Podcast Operations Manager in the subject line

• Why you believe you are the best candidate for this role

title: Remote Filipino Virtual Assistant – Medicare Turning 65 Lead Ninja & SOA Booking Wizard
description:
Location: 100% Remote (Philippines-based only – we want that signature Pinoy hustle & heart!)

About the Role

We’re a growing Medicare insurance team focused on helping people turning 65 secure the perfect healthcare coverage. We need a reliable, upbeat Filipino VA who treats every lead like gold and only books clean, high-quality Scope of Appointments (SOAs). No spam, no fake bookings, no no-shows — just real, show-up-ready appointments that help our agents close.

This is a performance-driven role. Junk gets you nothing. Quality gets you paid big.

What You’ll Do Every Day

•  Work warm & fresh leads for folks turning 65 (phone + text +
 combos).

•  Qualify properly: age, Medicare status, real interest, basic needs — friendly but firm.

•  Book compliant SOAs straight into calendar/CRM (full Medicare SOA rules training + scripts provided).

•  Keep follow-ups tight and logs clean.

•  Send short daily summaries.

•  Hit weekly targets while keeping attendance rates strong.

Requirements to Rock This

•  Excellent spoken & written English with a warm, professional phone presence.

•  1+ year in appointment setting, customer service, or outbound sales (insurance/Medicare exp = big plus).

•  Reliable high-speed internet, quiet workspace, laptop + headset.

•  Comfortable with Google tools, Calendly, basic CRMs.

•  Available for US Eastern Time shifts (evenings PH time – flexible once you prove consistency).

•  Honest, detail-oriented, and hates sending junk bookings.

Pay + The “Turning 65 Treasure Hunt” Incentive (Junk-Proof & Motivating)

Base Pay: $4–$5 USD per hour (based on experience & interview).

The Treasure Hunt – Quality Pays, Junk Pays Zero

We reward real results, not volume.

•  Base Commission: $4 USD per booked SOA.

•  Treasure Points System (gamified to keep you focused on quality):

 •  +1 point for every booked SOA.

 •  +10 bonus points if the prospect actually attends (no-show = 0 points).

 •  +15 extra points if the agent marks it “highly qualified / closed sale” (big win!).

Monthly Treasure Chests (Halved for leaner targets – still very achievable):

•  Bronze Chest – 30 points = $37.50 bonus

•  Silver Chest – 60 points +

title: Senior UX Designer
description:
Senior UX Designer

Part-Time Contract | ~39 weeks | Remote (Philippines)



-------

About Us



2 Dam Creative is an Australian digital agency with offices in Australia, the Philippines and the UK. We deliver large-scale web and mobile platforms for enterprise clients across government, health, membership organisations, and not-for-profit sectors. We are expanding our design team and looking for an experienced Senior UX Designer to join us on a specific project engagement.



----------

About the Role



This is a part-time, work-from-home contract running approximately 39 weeks. The total commitment is around 390 hours (roughly 49 working days) spread across the project lifecycle, with the workload varying by phase. You will be embedded within our Australian delivery team, working on a complex, multi-platform project that spans a responsive national website, a branded member mobile app, CRM integrations, and multiple third-party system touchpoints.



----------

Indicative Weekly Commitment



The heavier weeks are front-loaded. During the build phase, the role shifts to accessibility testing, design QA, and occasional UAT support.



39 weeks | ~390 hours / ~49 days



------------

What You Will Be Doing



 > Planning and conducting user research using qualitative and quantitative methods, then translating findings into clear, actionable recommendations

> Creating user personas, journey maps, information architecture, and interaction models that guide product strategy and design direction

> Designing intuitive, accessible interfaces for both web and mobile, with a strong focus on WCAG 2.1 AA+ compliance

> Building interactive prototypes and wireframes in Figma to communicate design intent to developers and stakeholders

> Producing developer-ready specifications, design systems, and component libraries that the build team can work from directly

> Collaborating with art directors, content specialists, developers, and project managers across Australian and international time zones

> Supporting accessibility and usability testing throughout the build and QA phases

> Mentoring junior designers and contributing to the continuous improvement of our UX processes and standards



------

What We Are Looking For



> At least five years of hands-on experience in UX research and design for digital products, ideally across both web and native mobile

> A degree in Human-Computer Interaction, Psychology, Design, or a related field (or equivalent professional experience)

> Strong working knowledge of WCAG accessibility standards and inclusive design principles

> Expert-level proficiency in Figma, including design systems, prototyping, auto-layout, and developer handoff workflows

> Experience working within complex integration environments where UX decisions need to account for CRM data, tiered access models, SSO, or payment flows

> Solid analytical skills with the ability to synthesise research data into recommendations that non-technical stakeholders can act on

> Confident communication and presentation skills, comfortable working with senior client stakeholders

> Self-motivated, reliable, and able to work independently in a remote environment while staying closely connected to the wider team

> A portfolio that demonstrates your end-to-end UX process, not just final visual outputs



-------

Nice to Have



> Experience with AI-assisted design tools such as Figma AI

> Familiarity with analytics and session recording tools (GA4, Hotjar, Mixpanel, or similar)

> Experience with content-heavy platforms requiring complex information architecture

> Previous work with Australian clients or within Australian digital agencies



--------

What We Offer



> Day shift hours: 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM Philippine time (on scheduled working days)

> Part-time contract, work from home

> Competitive day rate

> The opportunity to work on a genuinely interesting, large-scale project with a senior, collaborative team



-------

How to Apply



Submit your CV, portfolio, and a brief cover note explaining why this role is the right fit for you.



If you are shortlisted following the initial interview, the next stage will include a practical skills assessment to help us find the best match for the role.

title: Real Estate Lead Conversion Specialist
description:

About the Role

We are seeking a high-level Real Estate Virtual Assistant who will live inside our CRM and help drive lead conversion, appointment setting, and client relationship management. This is NOT a basic task-doer position. This is a growth-focused role for someone who thinks like a partner, understands conversion, and is motivated by performance and results.

You will directly support a Trusted Real Estate Advisor serving the Tampa Bay, Florida market with a goal of scaling to $4 million per quarter in gross sales volume.



About Shenille Ford – Trusted Real Estate Advisor

Shenille Ford is a Florida-based Realtor® serving Pasco, Pinellas, Hillsborough, Hernando, and Manatee counties. With over 5 years of experience, $40M+ in collective production, and hundreds of families served, she specializes in first-time homebuyers, sellers, and investors. She operates as a solo agent, a high-performance real estate powerhouse built on results, integrity, and relationship equity.

What We’re Looking For:

title: Guest Experience & Operations Coordinator (Short-Term Rental Company)
description:
WHO WE ARE:

We are a high-end exclusive boutique short-term rental (STR) and design/launch company managing and launching high value properties. Current portfolio includes 6 high performing properties with growth of an additional 10 by end of the year.



WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT:

In working with us, you can expect to find a supportive, fast-growing team that values clear communication, ownership, and pride in your work.  We trust our team to take initiative and solve problems, and we’re here to support you every step of the way.



WHO WE ARE LOOKING FOR: 

We are looking for someone who thinks fast, solves problems proactively, and takes full ownership of outcomes. Enjoys connecting and serving others through hospitality.  Takes pride in creating consistent 5-star guest experiences, transforming our guests into raving loyal fans by going above and beyond to ensure each stay is above expectations. 



YOUR OBJECTIVES:

- Own and deliver a consistent 5-star guest experience

- Build genuine, positive relationships with every guest

- Manage all guest communication with speed, warmth, and professionalism

- Ensure each home is fully guest-ready—going beyond minimum standards and tailored to each guest’s stay

- Take ownership of issues immediately and follow through to full resolution



You are the first responder and problem solver for the business, ensuring every guest feels taken care of from booking to checkout.  You are our reputation. 



DETAILED RESPONSIBILITIES FOR SUCCESS:



>Guest Communication (PRIMARY)

- Respond to all guest messages using:

   - Hospitable Management Software

- Maintain fast response times (goal: under 5 minutes during shift)

- Provide clear, friendly, and professional communication

- Turn issues into positive guest experiences



>Coordination & Execution

- Communicate directly with:

   - Cleaners

   - Maintenance vendors

   - Supply Sites (Walmart/Amazon, etc.)

- Handle real-time issues such as:

   - Cleaning problems

   - Maintenance issues

   - Missing or low supplies



>Ownership Rule (CRITICAL)

- If you identify a problem:

   - You take action immediately

   - You follow up consistently

   - You ensure the issue is COMPLETELY resolved

- Nothing is passed off without confirmation.



>Handoff to Systems Team

- After resolving issues, you will:

   - Share clear updates with the operations/systems team

   - Provide receipts, notes, and confirmations

   - Ensure all relevant information is shared for accurate and proper documentation



YOU ARE ---NOT--- RESPONSIBLE FOR: 

- Pricing or revenue strategy

- Listing optimization

- Invoicing or monthly reporting to clients/owners



YOUR PROVEN EXPERIENCE INCLUDES:

- Experience with short-term rental platforms:

   - Hospitable (Required)

   - Airbnb, Vrbo, and other OTA's (Online Travel Agencies)

- Experience coordinating cleaners, maintenance teams and managing supplies 

- Experience handling guest complaints or escalations



OTHER INHERENT SKILLS: 

- Excellent written English (friendly, clear, professional)

- Fast response and strong sense of urgency

- Strong problem-solving ability to create win-win solutions

- Ability to manage multiple conversations and tasks at once

- Highly reliable and organized



HOW TO APPLY



To be considered, you MUST follow ALL instructions:



1. Start your application with this exact phrase: "5 Star Guest Experience is my Experience"

2. Submit a 2–3 minute Loom video answering:

   - Tell us about yourself

   - Describe a time you handled a difficult customer or situation

   - How you stay organized when handling multiple urgent tasks

   - Answer this scenario:

“A guest messages at 6pm central time in US: ‘There are no towels and check-in was 1 hour ago.’

What EXACT steps would you take?”



3. Attach your resume



Thank you for taking the time to apply, look forward to connecting!

title: Web Designer & 3D Developer Needed – Premium AI Tech Agency Website with Live Voice Demo & Immersive Scroll Experience
description: I'm building a next-generation tech agency that helps businesses implement cutting-edge technology — starting with AI voice automation. I need a world-class website that not only looks incredible but features a live, interactive AI voice demo so visitors can experience the technology firsthand before ever speaking to me.?The brand name is still being finalised, but the vision is clear: a site that feels like it's from the future — but simple enough that anyone can use it.??The Hero Feature?The centrepiece of the website should be a live AI voice demo where visitors can actually call or interact with an AI voice agent directly on the page. My strong preference is Retell AI integration as it is the platform I use for my actual business — so the demo would be a real representation of what I offer clients. If Retell AI isn't feasible, the next preference is ElevenLabs, and if neither are possible I'm open to other alternatives — but Retell is the priority. Think of it like an interactive product demo that sells itself.??3D & Scroll Experience?I'd love to explore an immersive 3D scroll experience — for example, a 3D AI brain or neural network that animates and reacts as the user scrolls down the page, visualising the AI in action. I'm open to the designer's creative interpretation of this — if you have a better idea that achieves the same "wow" effect, pitch it to me.??Design Vision?The one non-negotiable: despite the cool visuals, the site must feel simple, clear and approachable. My target clients include trades businesses like plumbers and electricians who aren't tech-savvy. Nothing should feel confusing or overwhelming.?Built to be flexible — as new technology emerges, the site should be easy to update and repurpose beyond just AI voice.??Requirements??Strong portfolio of modern, high-end websites — please include links?Must have experience with 3D design and scroll-based animations?Experience with interactive or demo-driven websites a big plus?Experience with Retell AI or ElevenLabs integration a significant advantage?What timeframe would you need to complete this project?

title: Virtual assisant/Real estate
description:
Cold Calling Virtual Assistant – U.S. Real Estate Wholesaling



Overview



We are looking for a highly skilled and experienced Virtual Assistant specializing in **cold calling and lead qualification** within the U.S. real estate wholesaling market.



This role is focused on **quality over quantity**. You will be responsible for speaking with property owners, identifying serious selling opportunities, and delivering well-qualified leads to our acquisitions team.



We are looking for someone who can **think, not just follow a script**.



Key Responsibilities



**Cold Calling**



* Make outbound calls to property owners (lists provided)

* Build rapport quickly and naturally

* Identify motivation and uncover real selling intent

* Handle objections confidently and professionally



**Lead Qualification**



* Gather and confirm:



  * Property condition

  * Seller motivation

  * Timeline to sell

  * Price expectations

* Clearly assess lead quality (hot, warm, cold)



**CRM Management**



* Accurately input all lead details into CRM (Podio, REsimpli, GoHighLevel, etc.)

* Write clear, structured, and useful call notes

* Tag and organize leads properly

* Set follow-up reminders



**Follow-Up**



* Re-engage prospects when needed

* Build relationships with potential sellers over time



What is a Qualified Lead?



A qualified lead must include:



* Seller is genuinely considering selling

* Property details collected and verified

* Clear motivation identified

* Timeline to sell established

* Price expectation or openness to an offer

* Detailed notes entered in CRM



Requirements



* Proven experience in **cold calling for U.S. real estate wholesaling**

* Strong understanding of seller psychology and motivation

* Excellent English communication (clear, confident, natural tone)

* Ability to control conversations and build trust quickly

* Experience with CRM systems (Podio, REsimpli, GoHighLevel, etc.)

* Reliable internet and professional work setup



Compensation



* $4–$6/hour (based on experience and performance)

* $150–$300 bonus per closed deal you sourced



Performance Expectations



* Consistent daily call activity

* High-quality, well-documented leads

* Strong communication with acquisitions team

* Professional and organized CRM management



How to Apply



Please include:



1. Your experience with cold calling in real estate wholesaling

2. Tools and CRMs you’ve used

3. Your average daily call volume

4. A short voice recording (required)







Important



We are looking for **experienced callers only**.

If you do not have direct experience in U.S. real estate wholesaling, please do not apply.

title: Rockstar Executive Assistant
description:
Forget What You Think an EA Does.



This isn't a role for someone who manages a calendar and waits for instructions. This is a role for someone who sees what needs to happen before they're asked, and makes it happen before anyone notices it was a problem.



We're growing fast. The Founder is deep in client delivery, sales, expansion, and content simultaneously. To keep scaling without losing quality, we need one person who owns the operational and administrative infrastructure around him.

That's you. If you're ready.



***About Growth Dinners***



Growth Dinners operates and runs curated executive dinner experiences for B2B companies across multiple U.S. cities. Our clients are sponsors and hosts who use our dinners to access hard-to-reach buyers, build relationships with C-suite prospects, and accelerate pipeline in a room where business actually gets done.



We're a lean, founder-led operation with high standards, fast decisions, and a bias toward action. Every hire matters here.

What You'll Own



***Event & Dinner Operations***



Coordinate logistics for B2B executive dinners end-to-end — venues, RSVPs, run-of-show, day-of execution, and post-event follow-up

Build and maintain event timelines, checklists, and SOPs that reduce friction and prevent anything from falling through the cracks

Manage contractor communication and vendor coordination before and after each event

Track event deliverables and ensure all client commitments are met on schedule



*** Founder Calendar & Inbox***



Own the Founder's calendar — proactively protect high-output time, schedule with intent, and eliminate scheduling back-and-forth

Triage and manage inbox communications — draft responses, flag priorities, and handle routine correspondence independently

Prepare the Founder for every meeting with relevant context, agenda, and background

Follow up on open loops so nothing stalls due to a missed reply

Client Communications & Follow-Up

Draft and manage client-facing communications with professionalism and attention to brand voice

Track client deliverables, deadlines, and milestones across active engagements

Coordinate onboarding touchpoints for new dinner sponsors and ensure a smooth client experience from contract to event day

Send post-event summaries, attribution documentation, and follow-up sequences on behalf of the Founder



*** CRM & Pipeline Management***



Keep the CRM clean, updated, and actionable — log activity, update deal stages, and flag stale contacts

Manage outreach sequences and ensure follow-ups are sent on schedule

Build and maintain contact lists for prospective dinner guests and event sponsors

Surface weekly pipeline reports and flag anything that needs the Founder's attention



** Who You Are**



You're the kind of person who finishes something before being asked twice. You think in systems. You write with clarity. And you take ownership like it's your business — because in many ways, it will be.

You have 3–4 years of experience in an Executive Assistant, Operations Coordinator, or similar role — ideally in a fast-moving startup, agency, or events environment

You're a strong writer who can draft
s, client communications, and follow-ups that sound like a human wrote them.

Your AI is fluent and up to speed on how to use the latest AI tools like ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, etc. to maximize efficiency and productivity.

You understand basic automation tools like Zapier and 
 or are excited to jump in and to learn them on your first day.

You have a working knowledge of CRM tools & SOPs databases (HubSpot, Notion, Airtable, or similar) and don't need to be trained from zero.

You're proactive — you don't wait to be told the same thing twice

You communicate ahead of problems, not after

You've read Buy Back Your Time by Dan Martell or you're ready to — because that's the operating system we run on

You thrive in a remote environment and take ownership of your own structure and accountability



***What Success Looks Like in 30 Days***



The Founder's inbox and calendar are under control — no missed follow-ups, no double-bookings, no surprise deadlines

Every active dinner event has a documented run-of-show and client communication log

The CRM is clean, current, and being used to drive follow-up — not just store data

You've proactively identified and solved at least two recurring operational problems without being asked



***Our Hiring Process***

We move fast and we value your time. Here's exactly what to expect:

1. Submit a 1-minute Loom video answering: Why are you the right fit for this role? What's a system or process you built that made someone's work life meaningfully easier?

2. 15-minute intro call — quick vibe check on expectations and fit

3. Short skills assessment — we'll send you a realistic task (paid) to see how you think and execute

4. Final interview with the Founder

5. Offer — if we're both excited, we move immediately

Applications without a Loom video will not be reviewed.

This role is remote, part-time 10 hours per week with ability to grow into full-time role.



Compensation



Commensurate with experience. Range: $5.00–$7.50/hr or $200 USD - $300/month depending on scope and experience. This role starts part-time at 10 hours per week. 



We work together on a paid project, then move to part-time, and finally full-time after you've demonstrated you're a fit.



To apply, send your Loom video telling us why you're a fit + resume to 
 with subject line: "EA Application — [Your Name]"

title: Coding & Robotics Education Licensee - Master Trainer
description:
Coding & Robotics Education Licensee - Master Trainer







Remote-Based  |  Occasional Travel Required  |  Part-Time / Contract





About Our Company :



We are the leading Coding and Robotics Education Academy in Singapore. Our passion and mission is in teaching the children of today, the skills of tomorrow, and have fun learning it !



About the Role : 



We are seeking a passionate and experienced Coding & Robotics Education Trainer to support our growing network of licensees. The successful candidate will be responsible for training our licensee partners who have adopted our full coding and robotics curriculum, ensuring they are well-equipped to deliver high-quality STEM education programs.



This role is primarily remote-based, with occasional travel required for training sessions, workshops, or partner support.



You will empower our licensees with the knowledge, skills, and confidence to deliver our coding and robotics curriculum effectively to children aged 5 to 14 years old. You will also play a key role in keeping our curriculum fresh, relevant, and engaging by updating existing materials and developing new content as our programmes evolve.



Key Responsibilities :



•	Train and onboard new licensees on our full coding and robotics curriculum, ensuring they are equipped to deliver high-quality lessons.

•	Conduct training workshops and sessions for licensee educators, both virtually and in-person as required.

•	Provide ongoing coaching, mentoring, and support to licensees post-training.

•	Evaluate licensee competency and readiness to deliver the curriculum independently.

•	Review, update, and improve existing curriculum materials to ensure they remain current and effective.

•	Develop new curriculum modules and lesson plans aligned with industry trends and educational best practices.

•	Collaborate with the curriculum and product teams to align training delivery with programme goals.

•	Gather feedback from licensees and learners to continuously improve training programmes.

•	Maintain comprehensive training documentation, guides, and resources.

•	Travel occasionally to licensee locations for in-person training, support, and quality assurance.



Who We Are Looking For :



We are seeking someone who combines a love for education technology with real-world teaching experience. The ideal candidate:

•	Has proven experience training adults in a professional or educational setting.

•	Has hands-on experience teaching or working with children between the ages of 5 and 14 years old.

•	Holds a background in coding, robotics, STEM education, or a closely related field.

•	Is comfortable using and teaching tools such as Scratch, Python, block-based coding platforms, or robotics kits (e.g., LEGO Mindstorms, mBot, or similar).

•	Demonstrates strong curriculum development and instructional design skills.

•	Is an excellent communicator — able to simplify complex technical concepts for varied audiences.

•	Is self-driven, organised, and able to work independently in a remote environment.

•	Is flexible and willing to travel when required.



Qualifications & Experience :



•	Diploma or Degree in Education, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related discipline.

•	Minimum 2 years of teaching or training experience — experience with both children and adults is strongly preferred.

•	Current or former school teachers specialising in Computing, ICT, STEM, or related subjects are warmly encouraged to apply.

•	Experience in curriculum writing, lesson planning, or instructional design is a strong advantage.

•	Experience in a franchising or licensee-based education business is a bonus, but not required.

•	Proficiency in digital tools for remote training delivery (e.g., Zoom, Google Meet, LMS platforms).



What We Offer :



•	Flexible, remote-based working arrangement.

•	A meaningful role shaping the next generation of coders and innovators.

•	A collaborative and supportive team environment.

•	Opportunities for professional development and growth within the organisation.

•	Travel allowances for required in-person training engagements.

•	Competitive remuneration commensurate with experience.

title: Education Franchise Business Development Manager
description:
Business Development Executive

Department	Franchise & Licensing

Reports To	Head of Partnerships / CEO

Location	Remote (anywhere) — occasional travel may be required

Employment Type	Full-Time

Experience Required	Minimum 2 years in B2B sales, business development, or education industry



ABOUT RIPPLE CREATE

Ripple Create is a coding and robotics academy dedicated to nurturing the next generation of innovators, aged 5 to 18. Through hands-on, curriculum-aligned programmes, we empower young learners to design, build, and code — preparing them for a technology-driven future. We are rapidly expanding our reach through franchise and licensing partnerships across the region and beyond.



ROLE OVERVIEW

We are looking for a driven and self-motivated Business Development Executive to lead the growth of our franchise and licensing network. In this remote-based role, you will own the full partnership lifecycle — from generating and qualifying leads through marketing channels, to nurturing prospects, coordinating technical demonstrations, closing agreements, and supporting new partners through their first six months of operation. This is a high-impact, end-to-end commercial role ideal for someone with a passion for education and a proven track record in consultative sales.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Lead Generation & Marketing Channel Management

•	Manage and optimise franchise and licensing marketing portals, listings, and digital channels (e.g. franchise directories, social media, paid ads,
 campaigns).

•	Monitor inbound enquiries across all channels and respond promptly to maintain lead 
ntum.

•	Work with the marketing team to develop compelling collateral and messaging targeting prospective franchisees and licensees.



CRM Management & Lead Administration

•	Accurately enter, update, and maintain all leads and prospect interactions in the CRM system in a timely manner.

•	Track pipeline stages, flag at-risk leads, and maintain a clean, up-to-date database.

•	Generate regular reports on lead volume, conversion rates, and pipeline health for management review.



Lead Nurturing, Qualification & Sales Process

•	Proactively nurture prospects through personalised outreach, follow-up calls,
s, and virtual meetings.

•	Qualify leads against defined criteria (financial capacity, market fit, operational readiness) to prioritise pipeline.

•	Guide prospects through the sales journey, addressing objections and providing relevant information and case studies.

•	Coordinate and schedule technical demonstrations with the Sales Trainer, ensuring prospects are well-prepared and engaged.

•	Follow through on all stages of the deal process, working collaboratively toward signed agreements.



Post-Sales Partner Support (First 6 Months)

•	Serve as the primary point of contact for new franchise and licensing partners from signing through the first six months of operations.

•	Coordinate onboarding activities, training schedules, and resource access with internal teams.

•	Proactively check in with partners to resolve issues, gather feedback, and ensure a smooth launch.

•	Facilitate a structured handover to the Account Manager at the end of the six-month post-sales period.



REQUIREMENTS

•	Experience: Minimum 2 years of experience in B2B sales, business development, franchise development, or a related field; experience in the education sector is a strong advantage.

•	Sales Acumen: Demonstrated ability to manage a full sales cycle from lead generation to closing, with a consultative, relationship-first approach.

•	CRM Proficiency: Hands-on experience using CRM platforms (e.g. HubSpot, Salesforce, Zoho, or similar); disciplined in data entry and pipeline management.

•	Communication: Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English; ability to adapt tone and style for diverse stakeholders.

•	Organised & Self-Directed: Highly organised with strong time management skills; comfortable managing multiple prospects and tasks independently in a remote environment.

•	Tech Savvy: Comfortable with digital marketing channels, video conferencing tools, and productivity software.

•	Passion for Education: Genuine interest in children’s education, STEM, or technology learning programmes.



NICE TO HAVE

•	Experience in franchise development, licensing, or channel partnerships.

•	Background or network in the K-12 education, enrichment, or EdTech space.

•	Familiarity with coding, robotics, or STEM-related products or curricula.

•	Multilingual abilities to engage prospects across different markets.



WHAT WE OFFER

•	A fully remote, flexible work arrangement with a results-driven culture.

•	A meaningful role contributing to the growth of quality STEM education globally.

•	Competitive base salary with performance-based commissions and incentives.

•	Supportive team, clear processes, and the tools you need to succeed.

•	Opportunities for career growth as the franchise and licensing network expands.



HOW TO APPLY

Send your CV and a brief cover letter explaining why you’re excited about this role. Please include the subject line: “BD Manager — Franchise & Licensing”. We look forward to hearing from you.

title: Sales and Live Stream Associate
description:
Position: Sales and Live Stream Associate (Japan Operations)

Location: Remote

Salary: PhP 24,000 / month (Fixed)

Employment Type: Full-time

Working Hours: 10 AM to 7 PM JST, 5 days a week



ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for a high-energy, persuasive Sales and Live Stream Associate to join our team. You will be the "on-camera" face of our refurbished gadget business in Japan, specifically targeting the OFW market. While the product videos are prepared by our Japan team, your job is to "sell" them by bringing life to the specs, engaging with the audience, and converting viewers into buyers.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

• Live Sales Presentation: Host "Video Live" events using pre-recorded footage. You will provide the face-to-camera commentary, highlight gadget "flexes" (specs, condition, price), and maintain high energy to keep the audience engaged.

• Active Engagement: Monitor and reply to every comment during and after the live sessions. You are responsible for the "vibe" of the comment section.

• Sales Conversion (The Close): Proactively message customers who expressed interest. You will handle inquiries in the DMs, explain gadget conditions, and close the sale.

• Customer Support & Sales Admin: Assist with general customer service inquiries and coordinate with the team to ensure buyer information (address, contact details) is accurately recorded for shipping.



QUALIFICATIONS

• On-Camera Personality: You are charismatic, articulate in Tagalog/English, and can sell with confidence.

• Sales Drive: 1–2 years of experience in Sales or Customer Service. You must be comfortable "asking for the sale" and following up with leads.

• Gadget Knowledge: Basic understanding of smartphone and laptop specs (e.g., Battery Health, SSD vs HDD, RAM).

• Resilience: Ability to handle a fast-paced environment and manage multiple customer chats simultaneously.



WHY JOIN US

You’ll be a vital part of a streamlined team working directly with a Japan-based company. This role offers a guaranteed monthly service fee (not commission-based) and a remote work environment. You won't just be a voiceover; you will be the face and driver of our sales.



TO APPLY

Please send the following via OLJ message:

• Updated Resume (PDF preferred).

• A 1-Minute Sample Video: Use your phone to "sell" the phone you are currently holding. Show us your energy, how you "flex" the features, and how you’d invite a customer to "Mine" it.

• Screenshot of your current internet speed test.

• Photo of your work-from-home setup.

Upload the above in your cloud service provider (OneDrive, Google Drive, Dropbox, etc.) of choosing.
