title: Shopify Web Design & Site Maintenance
description:
IMPORTANT: READ BEFORE APPLYING!



You MUST read this entire post carefully, including the “How to Apply” section.

Applications that do not follow instructions will NOT be reviewed.



Rate: $4.00/hr starting

Type: Independent Contractor (Hourly, Weekly Invoicing)

Schedule: Multiple shifts available

• 12AM–8AM CST

• 8AM–4PM CST

• 4PM–12AM CST

(Central Standard Time)



Primary Role: Shopify  Web Design & Site Maintenance

Location: Remote (Philippines applicants welcome)



About the Role:



We are hiring a Shopify  Web Design & Site Maintenance who can stay alert, follow process, and also execute clean website work.



Your responsibility is website work :

• Web design, updating products, banners, pages, menus, etc.

• Making sure our branding, layouts, and promos are current and consistent



This is NOT a “watch Netflix in the background” job. We are looking for someone dependable, calm under pressure, and detail-focused who can handle both responsible monitoring and professional presentation of our online brands.



You will be trusted with sensitive access. Reliability is everything here.



Core Responsibilities



Web/Creative 

• Design, update, and maintain websites (Shopify, WordPress, or similar CMS)

• Create and upload homepage banners, landing pages, promo sections, etc.

• Keep brand look consistent (fonts, colors, message, tone) across sites

• Do light content edits (product descriptions, pricing, images, disclaimers)

• Make sure sites stay clean, mobile-friendly, and easy to navigate



Daily Ops / Communication

• Communicate with management through WhatsApp and

• Track hours accurately using Timeproof (this is required)

• Send weekly invoice on time for payment

• Follow escalation procedures exactly (no freelancing/guessing)



Minimum Requirements (Do NOT apply if you don’t have these)



• Excellent written and spoken English

• Proven experience in web design/website management (Shopify, WordPress, HTML/CSS, etc.)

• Quiet, distraction-free workspace (no TV, kids, loud background noise while on shift)

• Dual monitors (REQUIRED): one screen for security feeds, one screen for work

• Stable high-speed internet connection

• Reliable computer or laptop that can stream multiple live cameras smoothly

• Strong focus and honesty—you must actually be present and awake the entire shift

• Consistent attendance. If you disappear, you’re done.

• Comfortable following instructions and documenting what you did every shift



Preferred / “Nice to Have”



These will move you to the top of the list:



• Backup power (UPS, inverter, generator, etc.)

• Graphic/layout skills (Canva, Photoshop, Illustrator, etc.)

• Experience working overnight/US hours without issues



Tools You’ll Use



• Shopify —website edits and updates

• Canva / Photoshop – simple graphics, homepage banners, etc.

• Timeproof – time tracking / attendance verification

• WhatsApp – shift communication and escalation

• Dialpad – urgent escalation calls or voi
 monitoring

•
 – daily reports, i ---------- summaries, and admin communication



Compensation & Payroll



• $4.00/hour starting rate

• Paid weekly by invoice (independent contractor)

• Workweek is Monday–Sunday (flexible)

• Payments are sent every Monday after we review your invoice and hours

• This is hourly contractor work, not a salaried employee position



We reward people who show up every day, follow the process, and don’t cause problems. If you’re consistent and valuable, there is growth.



Who This Role Is (and Is NOT) For



You’re a fit if:

• You treat this like a real job, not “extra income while you multitask 3 other gigs.”

• You’re calm, observant, and not dramatic.

• You can update a website banner without breaking the entire layout.



You are NOT a fit if:

• You can’t work US time zones reliably

• You have unstable internet or no backup plan for power

• You disappear, ignore messages, or oversleep shifts

• You get bored easily and mentally “clock out” while still on the clock



We are only looking for teammates who will treat this seriously.



How to Apply (Follow Exactly)



Send ONE
 to 
 line:

Application – Shopify  Web Design & Site Maintenance  – [Your Name]



Your
 MUST include all of the following, or it will not be reviewed:



- Resume (PDF or DOC)

- Short introduction

- Tell us about your web design experience

- Shift availability in CST



Example: “I can work 12 AM–8 AM CST Monday–Friday.”

- Confirm your equipment

- Do you have dual monitors?

- Internet speed (upload/download)?

- Do you have backup power?



Final Note:



We are actively hiring. If you’re reliable, steady, and serious about long-term work, we want to hear from you.

title: Rockstar Paralegal Assistant
description:
Schedule: 9 AM - 5PM in the Philippines Times



We are seeking a meticulous, tech-savvy Virtual Legal Assistant to serve as the backbone of our digital organization. Your primary mission will be to ensure that every client file is complete, every data point is accurate, and our case management system reflects the current status of every matter. This is a remote, full-time position requiring extreme attention to detail and a "measure twice, cut once" mentality. Also - we are one big team looking for a long term hire to grow with us. You will be part of our team and we will meet with you regularly by zoom, with camera turned on because we want to maintain human connection with all tea
mbers.



Key Responsibilities:



Data Entry/Organization: Inputting sensitive client information into our case management software with accuracy. Names, addresses. You will make sure this information is entered in multiple places. You will review scanned documents and you will interpret from those documents where certain information should be entered.



Digital File Architecture: Organizing and maintaining cloud-based client folders (e.g., Dropbox) according to our firm’s specific naming conventions and structure.



Workflow Tracking: Updating project milestones and deadlines to ensure no client file "stalls" during the drafting or funding process. We are doing all of the work - you will help us make sure the current status of the matter is always correctly documented.



SOPs: You will create the SOPs for your own job. We will give you instructions and guidelines, but you will also create your own SOPs for how you approach the work.



AI Image Generation: You will help us format marketing materials, help us create AI educational images, etc. You will help us with Canva materials as well.



Legacy Data Cleanup: Assisting with the migration or reorganization of older files into our current streamlined system.



Qualifications & Skills:



Legal Experience: 2+ years as a legal assistant or paralegal (Estate Planning experience is a significant plus).



Software Mastery: Proficiency with legal case management software (e.g., Lawcus, Legacy Architects, Wealth Counsel) and secure cloud storage systems.



Data Integrity: A proven track record of handling high-volume data entry with zero-to-minimal error rates.



Technical Setup: Must have a secure, high-speed internet connection and a dedicated, quiet workspace that complies with client confidentiality (NDAs will be required).



Reliability: You are someone who values "rugged" reliability—you show up, do the work to a high standard, and don't require micro-management once a process is set.



How to Apply:



Please submit your resume and a brief cover letter. In your letter, please mention one specific tool or method you use to double-check your own work for data entry errors.

title: Director of Database Management & Client Care (Real Estate Conversion Specialist)
description:
ROLE OVERVIEW

We are seeking a high-performance Inside Sales Agent (ISA) with elite CRM management skills to act as the primary engine for lead conversion and the logistical hub for our real estate operations. In this role, you own the "Voice of the Business" and are responsible for turning raw interest into set appointments through aggressive outbound calling, meticulous follow-up, and CRM automation. You will manage the entire lifecycle of property showings and agent feedback loops while maintaining total data integrity.



WORKING HOURS: 11:00 AM – 7:00 PM PST



DAILY WORKFLOW



System Audit & Lead Sync (11:00 AM – 1:00 PM):

- Audit lead generation from our VA who is doing the generation in new ad leads and engage with "hand-raisers" from social media comments/DMs to capture phone numbers.

- Address all GoHighLevel (GHL) responses and clean pipeline statuses specifically based on transaction data provided by our transaction coordinator (Director of TC).



Production, Newsletter & Logistics (1:00 PM – 4:00 PM):

- Showing Management: Coordinate with agents to schedule property access and ensure a seamless tour experience.

- Feedback Loops: Call every agent who accessed a listing for raw feedback; compile these notes into the "Weekly Seller Report" and send it to Graeham to manage seller strategy.

- Marketing Assembly: Receive high-end visual assets from video editor/content creator and strategic hooks to assemble and segment newsletter blasts in GHL.



Prime Time Conversion (4:00 PM – 7:00 PM):

- Execute outbound calls to hot leads and past clients while they are at home.

- The "Bridge Call" Protocol: You must initiate a call or text within 5 minutes of a recipient clicking a property link more than twice or a new lead syncing into the system.



The Closing & Review Loop:

- Wait 48 hours post-closing to confirm the client received their gift (wine or oil/vinegar).

- Ask and send them a link to do a review after closing.

- Execute follow-up check-ins 2–4 weeks post-close to ensure the client is settled.



APPLICATION REQUIREMENTS (MANDATORY)

1. Voice Recording: Attach a Loom or Vocaroo link introducing yourself. We require tonal authority and professional, clear English. As the "Voice of the Business," your ability to build trust over the phone is your primary asset.

2. Success Metrics & Experiments: Provide a summary of your lead conversion experience. We want to see how you have tested different protocols (e.g., speed-to-lead times or CRM tagging strategies) to increase appointment set rates.

3. Conflict Resolution Scenario: In your application, explain exactly how you would handle two showing agents demanding the same 2:00 PM Saturday slot for a hot listing without escalating the friction to someone else.



TECHNICAL SKILL STACK

- GoHighLevel (GHL): Advanced proficiency in segmentation, tagging, and pipeline hygiene.

- Any.do & Box: Familiarity with workspace monitoring; we operate on a system where no task is marked "Complete" without verified proof of the asset or logged call. We can teach you this.

- Climbo: Experience with review management is a plus (we will train you on our specific review-capture script).



COMPENSATION

- Hourly Rate: Negotiable based on experience and verified conversion skill.

- Incentives: Bonus per closing starting at $40, with potential for performance-based increases as you hit conversion milestones.

title: Youtube Video Editor
description:
Job Description:

We are looking for a talented and detail-oriented video editor to help create high-quality, engaging videos. The ideal candidate should have experience working with raw footage from phone recordings and be able to enhance content through expert editing, seamless transitions, and creative additions.



What You'll Do:

1. Tailored Workflow & Enhanced Quality: Optimize raw footage with noise reduction, audio leveling, and effects to elevate overall content quality.

2. Precise Editing & Efficiency: Edit and enhance videos while ensuring quick turnaround times without compromising quality.

3. Professional Video Editing: Deliver precise and creative edits to match the project’s needs.

4. Post-Production: Provide comprehensive post-production services to ensure polished, professional results.

5. Color Correction: Apply color grading and correction to enhance the visual appeal of videos.

6. Sound Design: Add background music, improve audio quality, and create an immersive audio experience.

7. Creative Storytelling: Make decisions on where to insert B-rolls, animations, zoom transitions, and graphics to make videos more engaging.

8. Subtitles & Captions: Add subtitles for accessibility and engagement.

9. Organizing Clips & Footage: Structure videos from beginning to end with a seamless flow.

10. Transitions & Video Effects: Implement smooth transitions and effects for a professional touch.

11. Intro & Outro: Create engaging intros and outros to enhance brand identity.

12. Multiple Revisions: Open to feedback and revisions to achieve the desired final product.



Ideal Candidate:



- Experienced in editing phone-recorded content

- Creative and detail-oriented

- Understands sound design and background music selection

- Proficient in color grading and correction

- Skilled in using professional video editing software (Premiere Pro, DaVinci Resolve, Final Cut Pro, etc.)

- Efficient and deadline-driven



If you're passionate about video editing and can transform raw footage into engaging, high-quality content, we'd love to hear from you!



To apply, please send:

1. Examples of past video editing work

2. Are you willing to sign an NDA before getting the materials to edit?

3. Your typical turnaround time for a 3-5 minute video

4. Are you willing to create a short sample? We will provide raw footage for the test.

title: Amazon Compliance Specialist
description:
Regulatory & Legal Compliance



Conduct in-depth research on FDA, EPA, FTC, and other relevant federal and state regulations.

Ensure compliance with international product safety standards (EU, UK, Canada, Asia-Pacific) when applicable.

Perform ongoing monitoring of regulatory updates and interpret implications for product categories.

Support intellectual property due diligence to prevent patent, copyright, or trademark infringement.



Risk Management & Product Compliance



Evaluate new and existing products for compliance risks and advise stakeholders on mitigation strategies.

Develop compliance testing protocols and coordinate with third-party labs and certification agencies.

Manage corrective actions, recalls, or withdrawals in alignment with Amazon’s policies and legal requirements.

Conduct audits of product listings and seller submissions to prevent unlawful or restricted product selection.



Cross-Functional Collaboration



Partner with Product Development, Supply Chain, and Seller Performance teams to execute compliance initiatives.

Provide clear guidance to vendors, suppliers, and internal teams on compliance requirements.

Train cross-functional stakeholders on compliance best practices and emerging regulatory risks.

Act as a subject matter expert to business teams during product launches, regulatory reviews, and inspections.



Policy & Process Development



Draft and implement compliance procedures, standard operating protocols (SOPs), and escalation processes.

Support automation and system-based compliance controls to scale monitoring and enforcement.

Contribute to internal policy development regarding restricted products, product claims, and safety certifications.

Continuously improve workflows to enhance accuracy, timeliness, and audit readiness.



Documentation & Reporting



Maintain accurate records of compliance assessments, approvals, and regulatory correspondence.

Prepare detailed reports and risk assessments for leadership review.

Provide supervisors with regular feedback on task progress, emerging risks, and resolution status.

Ensure confidentiality of sensitive data, supplier information, and Amazon business strategy.





Experience interacting directly with regulators including federal and state agencies

Experience in research and analyzing federal and state regulations

Familiarity and experience in dealing with FDA regulation is REQUIRED

Familiarity with international compliance frameworks (REACH, RoHS, CE marking).

Experience in compliance auditing, regulatory affairs, or legal review.

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail.

Excellent communication skills for engaging with global teams and regulators.

Ability to handle multiple projects in a fast-paced environment with strict deadline

title: Operations Assistant
description:
WORK ARRANGEMENT

Monday - Friday: 9:00 AM-6:00 PM, PH Time

Saturday: 9:00 AM-1:30 PM, PH Time



II. Salary: $400/month



ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for a detail-oriented and proactive Operations Assistant with strong Google Sheets / Excel skills to support day-to-day business operations, including HR processes, manage internal systems, and assist with technical setups.



JOB SCOPE

I. HR & Coordination

    1. Create and maintain Google Sheets trackers (e.g. payroll, internal dashboards)

    2. Coordinate onboarding of new hires (VAs)

    3. Conduct onboarding calls and guide tool setup

    4. Track attendance, leaves, and working hours

    5. Process payroll data every cutoff

    6. Respond to internal HR-related questions



II. Tech & Tool Support

    1. Help tea
mbers with tools and technical setups

    2. Coordinate system requests across teams



III. General Support

    1. Assist Operations Manager with ad hoc operational requests

    2. Coordinate with team leads and internal stakeholders



IV. Requirements

    1. Strong Google Sheets skills (comfortable with formulas and organizing data)

    2. Highly organized and detail-oriented

    3. Good written and spoken English

    4. Able to handle multiple tasks and priorities

    5. Proactive and able to follow through without constant supervision



V. Bonus (Nice to Have)

    1. Experience in HR or employee management

    2. Familiarity with LLMs (e.g., Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini)

    3. Basic technical knowledge (VPS, domains, automation tools)



VI. Requirements (MUST HAVE)

    1. Advanced Google Sheets / Excel skills and able to work with complex formulas such as:

        ARRAYFORMULA, QUERY, FILTER, VLOOKUP / XLOOKUP, IMPORTRANGE, IFERROR / nested logic, REGEX functions, Date-based calculations

    2. Able to debug and improve existing spreadsheet systems

    3. Strong logical thinking and problem-solving ability



Technical Requirements

CPU - i5 or better

Memory (RAM) - 16GB (minimum), 32GB or more (ideal)

Internet Speedtest - 50+ Mbps (download) | 20+ Mbps (upload)

Note: Stable electricity and internet connection is required (with a backup plan to ensure uninterrupted work)

title: Shopify Store Manager
description:
Dear,



We are looking for an experienced and driven Shopify Store Manager to lead our frontend operations full-time. In 2025, we generated $12M+ in revenue and are continuing to scale as a fast-growing fashion dropshipping company. We are looking for a hands-on leader who can manage a team, optimize performance, and take full ownership of our frontend operations.



About the Role:

You will be responsible for leading a team of 10 people, including product listers, media buyers, video editors, ads launchers, and photo editors. You will oversee the full frontend operation from store performance and conversion rate optimization to ad creatives and new store builds.



MUST HAVE EXPERIENCE WITH:



- Shopify (store management, store builds, CRO)

- Fashion dropshipping (managing stores in this niche specifically)

- Frontend operations (ads, creatives, funnels, product pages)

- Leading and coordinating a marketing/creative team



Responsibilities:



1) Managing and leading a team of 10 across multiple frontend departments.

2) Overseeing product listing, ad creative production, and media buying.

3) Optimizing conversion rates across all active stores.

4) Building and launching new Shopify stores from the ground up.

5) Tracking KPIs across departments and improving processes continuously.

6) Coordinating between media buyers, editors, and product listers to ensure aligned output.

7) Identifying bottlenecks and implementing systems to improve team efficiency.



Applicants must:



- Have proven experience managing Shopify stores in a fashion or dropshipping context.

- Have experience leading a team of at least 5+ people.

- Be available full-time and during weekends

- Be organized, process-driven, and results-oriented.

- Be available to start immediately or within short notice.



Application Instructions:



If you believe you are a good fit, please apply with a short introduction video of yourself (be original, do not read from a script).



Answer the following questions in your application (not in your video):



1) How many years of experience do you have with Shopify?

2) What was your most recent role, and what were your day-to-day responsibilities?

3) What type of e-commerce or dropshipping stores did you manage (markets, niches, number of stores)?

4) Do you have experience with Klaviyo? If yes, describe how you have used it.

5) Have you managed or built Shopify stores in the fashion niche specifically?

6) Can you describe your experience with frontend operations (ads, creatives, funnels, product pages)?

7) What are your experiences with Meta and Google ads?

8) Are you available full-time?

9) Are you available on weekends if needed?

10) Do you currently have other jobs or clients?

11) When can you start?



We are looking for someone who takes full ownership, leads with clarity, and consistently drives results.



I look forward to your proposal!



Best regards,

Tess

title: Influencer & Partnerships Coordinator (E-commerce Specialty Coffee Brand) *Part Time | Remote (Asia Time Zone)
description:
Innovative global coffee brand inventing one-of-a-kind tools redefining how coffee is experienced. We are looking for an Influencer & Partnerships Coordinator to build meaningful relationships across the coffee industry that support global campaigns and product launches.







ABOUT ICOSA BREWHOUSE:

ICOSA Brewhouse is a globally recognized specialty coffee brand reshaping coffee culture around the world. (
  / 
 )



We design one-of-a-kind tools that elevate everyday rituals and introduce new ways of enjoying coffee.



From record-breaking Kickstarter launches to collaborations with world-class coffee professionals, our products are used by champions, top cafés, and homebrewers across 100+ countries.



We are a design-led, innovation-driven brand operating in a niche we are actively shaping. We build thoughtfully, test deliberately, and care deeply about quality — in both product and execution.



As we continue to innovate, launch new products, and grow, we are looking for someone who will support in building and nurturing meaningful partnerships across the specialty coffee industry.



You will work directly with the founder and play an active role in shaping how ICOSA connects with competitors, industry leaders, and the coffee community globally.







YOUR ROLE AND OPPORTUNITY ON THE TEAM:

This is not a generic influencer outreach role.



You will be responsible for identifying, reaching out to, and developing relationships with coffee champions, competitors, industry leaders, and key opinion leaders (KOLs) within the specialty coffee community.



??You will help us:

• Identify and research relevant coffee professionals, coffee competitors, brands, and collaborators aligned with ICOSA’s Mission

 • Initiate thoughtful, personalized outreach on a daily basis in a way that fosters long-term relationships (not transactional exchanges)

 • Maintain a structured partnership pipeline and track all conversations inside our CRM system

 • Follow up proactively and ensure promising conversations are never lost

 • Coordinate collaboration discussions and next steps with clarity and organization

 • Provide weekly structured updates summarizing active conversations, progress, and upcoming opportunities

Over time, as trust and capability are demonstrated, there may be opportunities to expand scope into broader partnership coordination and system development.



What success looks like:

 • High-quality industry relationships are initiated and nurtured on a daily basis

 • Communication with KOLs is clear, thoughtful, and aligned with brand positioning

 • Regular improvements are made to the communication flow and strategy to improve engagement

 • Outreach efforts are organized and accurately tracked in a CRM

 • Promising conversations do not get lost or forgotten

 • Founders are supported daily update reports 



This role sits at the intersection of relationship-building and structured execution.



What this role is NOT:

• Not a sales quota-driven position

• Not limited to short-term campaign outreach

• Not a purely social media engagement role

• Not a transactional affiliate outreach or mass-DM automation role



This is relationship building inside a growing global brand.







ABOUT YOU / WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR:

You are someone who:

 • Communicates clearly and confidently in written English

 • Understands how to build rapport without being pushy

 • Is socially intelligent and able to read tone and context

 • Is organized and follows through consistently using established processes

 • Can represent a premium brand professionally

 • Is comfortable working directly with founders in a small, fast-moving team



Must have skills or experience:

• 1–4 years experience in outreach, influencer marketing, partnerships, or community roles

• Strong, fluent written communication skills in English

• Is comfortable initiating conversations with high-level industry professionals

• Is naturally organized and detail-oriented

• Comfortable tracking multiple conversations and follow-ups without being reminded

• Takes pride in clear, polished communication

• Comfort working directly with founders in a small, fast-moving team

• Enjoys relationship building, but approaches it with structure

• Curious about how partnerships are built inside growing companies



Big pluses:

 • Familiarity with specialty coffee culture (ex. you know what makes Panama Geisha special)

 • Experience engaging with Instagram creators or industry experts in a niche

 • Experience building CRMs or improving existing workflows







WHY JOIN US:

At ICOSA Brewhouse, you will:

• Work directly with founders who are deeply involved in product design and global campaigns.

• Contribute to campaigns that reach a global coffee audience.

• Operate close to innovation. See products and launches before they are introduced to the world.

• Be part of a team that is pioneering global coffee culture.



ou’ll be joining a close-knit team focused on building something meaningful through collaboration.



We care deeply about growth,not just as a company, but as individuals. As we expand, there are real opportunities to take on broader ownership based on performance and initiative.



If that sounds exciting to you, you will feel at home here.







COMPENSATION AND WORKING STRUCTURE

• Hours: Begins as Part Time (10-15 hours per week), with potential to expand based on performance and ownership

• Fully remote. Asia time zone working hours required.

• You will collaborate directly with one of the founders

• Compensation range: $3.50-$6.00/hour (based on relevant experience and demonstrated capability)





HOW TO APPLY



Are you ready? 



If you’d like to join us on our journey and grow together with us, we’re looking forward to meeting you.



We understand you may have a busy schedule, so we’ve made our application process easy to follow. 



To apply, please follow the steps below:

1. Complete the quick application: 
 

2. In the first question of the application start it with: "I’m (insert your name) from (insert your city/town) and my favourite food is (insert your favourite food). Then introduce yourself and why you think you’ll be a good fit for this position. 

3. We’ll be in touch with next steps for the interview once we receive it.

title: Senior Social Media & Marketing Manager (D2C E-commerce Coffee Brand) *Full Time | Remote (Asia Time Zone)

Innovative global coffee brand inventing one-of-a-kind tools redefining how coffee is experienced. Seeking a Marketing Manager to own campaigns, launches, and cross-channel execution alongside founders.





ABOUT ICOSA BREWHOUSE:

ICOSA Brewhouse is a globally recognized specialty coffee brand redefining how coffee is experienced. We are shaping coffee culture around the world. (
  / 
 )



We design and invent one-of-a-kind tools that didn’t exist before — tools that elevate everyday rituals and introduce new ways of enjoying coffee.



From record-breaking Kickstarter launches to collaborations with world-class coffee professionals, our products are used by champions, in top cafés, and in homes in 100+ countries around the world. 



We are a design-led, innovation-driven brand operating in a niche we are actively shaping. We build thoughtfully, test deliberately, and care deeply about quality — in both product and execution.



As we continue to innovate, launch new products, and grow, we are looking for a Senior Social Media & Marketing Manager to take ownership of how our work is brought to life across channels.

).

 campaigns aligned with launches, promotions, and collaborations.

, and paid efforts.

• Over time, take increasing ownership of campaign structuring, cross-channel coordination, partnership campaign oversight, and creative alignment across performance-driven efforts.

What success looks like:

• Social and
 channels operate smoothly with consistent execution and strong brand alignment.

What This Role Is Not:

This is a senior marketing operator position inside a growing global brand.







ABOUT YOU / WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR:

• Can translate ideas into clear execution plans.

• Is comfortable owning delivery without hand-holding.

• Is comfortable reviewing performance data and using insights to inform execution decisions.

• Is strategic enough to think ahead — but practical enough to execute consistently.



You are strategic enough to think ahead — but practical enough to execute consistently.



Must have skills or experience:

• Experience executing structured marketing campaigns.

• Experience within D2C or product-based brands strongly preferred.

• Comfortable operating inside e-commerce environments.

• Highly detail-oriented and disciplined in follow-through.

Big pluses:

• Experience coordinating freelancers or creative collaborators.

• Familiarity with Klaviyo or similar
 platforms.

• Exposure to AI-assisted marketing workflows.







WHY JOIN US:

At ICOSA Brewhouse, you will:

• Operate close to innovation — seeing products and launches before they are introduced to the world.

• Contribute to campaigns that reach a global coffee audience.

• Have real ownership inside a small, high-performing team where your work is visible and impactful.

We are a fully remote team — but we operate with structure and discipline.



You’ll be joining a close-knit group focused on building something meaningful with collaboration and accountability. There is no office politics. No unnecessary hierarchy. Just people committed to high standards.



We care deeply about growth — not just as a company, but as individuals. As we expand, there are real opportunities to take on broader ownership, lead initiatives, and shape how our marketing evolves.



If you want a role where:

title: [Urgent Hiring] Virtual Procurement Admin – WFH (Contractual - Renewable)
description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

Negentropy Limited is a Hong Kong–based social marketing agency operating in the fast-paced e-commerce space. We work with performance-driven teams focused on execution, speed, and results.



We are not a “chill” company. We are a high-accountability environment where people are expected to deliver, improve, and take ownership daily.



THE ROLE:

We are hiring a detail-obsessed, highly reliable Virtual Procurement Admin who can support daily operations, ensure accuracy, and keep execution on track.



This role is for someone who:

- Takes ownership without being chased

- Communicates clearly and proactively

- Handles confidential information with integrity

- Thrives in a fast-moving, performance-based environment

If you need constant reminders, supervision, or excuses to function — this role is not for you.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

- Monitor stock levels and identify purchasing needs

- Research, evaluate, and coordinate with potential vendors and suppliers

- Track purchase orders and ensure timely delivery of goods

- Update internal databases with order details (dates, vendors, quantities, pricing, and discounts)

- Conduct market research to identify pricing trends and cost-saving opportunities

- Negotiate with vendors to secure competitive and cost-effective deals

- Prepare and analyze cost and procurement reports

- Maintain accurate records of invoices, purchase orders, and contracts

- Follow up with suppliers to confirm, adjust, or resolve order concerns

- Coordinate with warehouse or internal teams to ensure items arrive in good condition



STANDARDS & WORK STYLE:

Our team operates on trust, clarity, and consistent execution. People who thrive here tend to naturally demonstrate:

- Integrity in Communication – Sharing accurate updates, even when things are not perfect

- Consistency – Showing up prepared, on time, and dependable across days—not just on good days

- Ownership Mindset – Following through on commitments and proactively closing gaps without being chased

- Structured Work Habits – Comfort with documented workflows, tracking systems, and clear visibility of work progress

- Professional Discipline – Maintaining a stable work environment and managing responsibilities with minimal oversight

- This role suits individuals who value clarity, accountability, and steady performance over time.



APPLICATION FIT:

- Candidates who tend to do well in this role are those who:

- Prefer clear expectations and structured processes 

- Take pride in delivering complete, accurate work 

- Communicate early when challenges arise (rather than after deadlines) 

- Are comfortable having their work reviewed and measured against standards 



On the other hand, this role may feel restrictive for those who:

- Prefer highly flexible or loosely defined work setups 

- Work best without structured tracking or accountability systems 

- Find it challenging to maintain consistency in attendance or output 

- Rely on rushed, unreviewed, or templated outputs instead of careful execution 



QUALIFICATIONS:

- Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration or any related field (fresh graduates are welcome)

- Experience in Purchasing, Procurement, or a similar role is an advantage

- Proficient in Google Sheets (comfortable with formulas and data handling)

- Strong English communication skills (written and verbal)

- Detail-oriented with good documentation and organizational skills

- Able to manage multiple tasks, analyze information, and resolve issues effectively

- Team-oriented with strong coordination and listening skills

- Practical, logical, and goal-driven in approach



TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS:

- Stable internet connection (minimum 50 - 100 Mbps)

Laptop/PC with at least:

- 8GB RAM

- Intel i5 (or equivalent) or higher

- 256GB storage or higher

For Mac Users:

- 8GB unified memory or more

- Apple M1 chip or newer (or Intel i5 and above)

- 256GB SSD or more



SALARY: ?18,000–?22,000 per month



WORK SCHEDULE & SETUP:

- Employment Type: Contractual

- Contract Term: 6 months (renewable based on performance and business needs)

- Work Setup: Remote 

- 5 working days per week with 2 rest days

- 8 working hours per day with a 1-hour lunch break

- Schedule may vary depending on business needs

- Must have own laptop and a reliable internet connection

title: Customer Experience & Operations Associate (E-commerce Coffee Company) *Full Time, Remote | Long-Term Growth Opportunity
description:
Are you ready…

To start a role that motivates and excites you?

And a position where we invest in your learning, education, and growth?



Then keep reading, we want to work with you!





WHY YOU’LL LOVE THIS POSITION:

This is not your typical support role — you’ll work directly with our founders, influence company-wide systems, and gain exposure to every part of the business.



Incredible company culture:

• Small, close team with collaborative, friendly and open culture.

• We believe the biggest asset of a company is our people. We focus on helping each tea
mber reach their full potential by investing in their learning, growth, skills, and education. When we grow as individuals, we succeed as a team.

• Not a fan of office politics or drama? We’re on the same page. Instead of wasting time with that, we focus on solving problems and reaching goals

• 100% remote, enjoy the comfort and flexibility of working from home



Pay, benefits, progression, and long-term growth:

• Competitive salary

• Benefits including up to 16 days a year Paid Time off

• Progression as we grow: greater responsibilities, leadership opportunities, and increased pay.

• Feel empowered, make decisions, have an impact

• Grow with the company and live up to your potential!

• Join an exciting, fast-growing company!







ABOUT US:

We’re a young, ambitious, and small (but growing!) company that’s making a massive impact in ecommerce. 



Since 2015 we’ve been obsessed with building award-winning products and awesome brands that people fall in love with. We have 2 primary brands in our portfolio: 

(1) an international award-winning coffee products brand 
 / 
).

(2) an educational coaching program that helps entrepreneurs grow their businesses to create a positive impact in the world!



Our two recent flagship products became the world’s most successful launches in their categories. We’ve shipped more than 200,000 orders worldwide and we’re continuing to expand.



Our culture is transparent and candid. You’re always welcome (and encouraged!) to ask questions or express a difference of opinion. Learning and growth is a cornerstone of our culture, both personally and professionally. We work hard, and we have fun!







WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR:

We’re looking for a proactive and detail-oriented team player to fill this role—someone who thrives on solving problems, staying organized, and making things run smoothly across departments.



Do you love helping customers, supporting a team, and keeping operations organized? Do you get satisfaction from making systems work better and “getting things done”? If yes, we want you on our team!



You’re probably the type of person who:

• Enjoys supporting others and finding solutions to problems

• Has strong communication and organizational skills

• Likes working behind the scenes to make sure everything runs like clockwork

• Is excited to wear multiple hats and be part of a growing, fast-moving company

• Has worked directly with founders or in a startup, and enjoys that fast-paced, flexible vibe

We’re not just looking for someone who can support our team, we’re looking for someone to be proactive with taking ownership of tasks, solve problems independently, and grow into larger responsibilities over time.







YOUR OPPORTUNITY AND ROLE ON THE TEAM:

Our culture is collaborative, friendly, and open. We hire people who are smart, ambitious, and have a strong desire to learn and grow.



Sound like you? This is an exciting long-term opportunity!



This is a fully remote position. You’ll work directly with our founders and collaborate with our small but mighty team to support customer happiness, streamline operations, and take on projects that help the business grow. 



This is a foundational role — one where you’ll gain visibility into all parts of the company.



As you grow, your role will grow with you, based on your interests and strengths:

• Want to work improving our systems and processes? Awesome!

• Have ideas on how to improve our website? Fantastic!

• Want to learn how to manage projects? Let’s go!



We’re not just hiring for a role — we’re hiring for potential. 



Your responsibilities will span 4 key areas: customer experience (30-40%), operations/logistics (20-30%), executive support (10-20%), and special projects (10-20%) — giving you exposure across the business. These percentages are estimates and you can expect your day-to-day to shift and evolve as the business grows and as you take ownership. 



1. Customer Experience

• Provide
/chat-based support for our customers across our channels (Shopify, Amazon, and wholesale)

• Answer order and product questions, resolve issues promptly, and ensure customers feel heard and supported

• Support wholesale partners with order updates, invoicing, and coordination



2. Order Management & Platform Coordination

• Manage, monitor, and troubleshoot orders across Shopify, Amazon, and wholesale channels

• Troubleshoot and resolve shipping or fulfillment issues with partners and customers

• Maintain accurate product listings, inventory data, and logistics information across platforms



3. Executive & Admin Support

• Manage founder and team inboxes, schedule meetings, and organize internal documentation

• Assist with travel bookings, meeting preparation, and note-taking

• Support small projects by tracking timelines, managing tasks, and ensuring follow-through



4. Special Projects & Company Operations

• Conduct research projects to support decision-making (tools, systems, competitors)

• Support lead generation efforts through identifying new wholesale prospects, brand partners, or influencers

• Support planning and execution of events, expos, or marketing campaigns — manage timelines, logistics, and tasks

• Document and maintain SOPs for operational clarity and training

• Test and troubleshoot web pages, workflows, or customer journeys to ensure quality and functionality

• Take ownership of ad hoc “figure it out” tasks — we’ll guide you, but we value initiative and independent problem-solving mindset



You’ll be trained on all platforms and tools, and we’ll support your learning — but we’re looking for someone who’s excited to take initiative and build systems along the way. This role evolves — as you grow, so will your responsibilities.







ABOUT YOU / REQUIRED SKILLS OR EXPERIENCE:

We value curiosity, reliability, and the ability to take ownership. If you enjoy figuring things out, learning on the job, and taking initiative, you’ll thrive here. This role is not a fit if you need constant direction or struggle to manage your own time.



Must have experience:

description:
Are you ready…

To start a role that motivates and excites you?

And a position where we invest in your learning, education, and growth?



Then keep reading, we want to work with you!





WHY YOU’LL LOVE THIS POSITION:

This is not your typical support role — you’ll work directly with our founders, influence company-wide systems, and gain exposure to every part of the business.



Incredible company culture:

• Small, close team with collaborative, friendly and open culture.

• We believe the biggest asset of a company is our people. We focus on helping each tea
mber reach their full potential by investing in their learning, growth, skills, and education. When we grow as individuals, we succeed as a team.

• Not a fan of office politics or drama? We’re on the same page. Instead of wasting time with that, we focus on solving problems and reaching goals

• 100% remote, enjoy the comfort and flexibility of working from home



Pay, benefits, progression, and long-term growth:

• Competitive salary

• Benefits including up to 16 days a year Paid Time off

• Progression as we grow: greater responsibilities, leadership opportunities, and increased pay.

• Feel empowered, make decisions, have an impact

• Grow with the company and live up to your potential!

• Join an exciting, fast-growing company!







ABOUT US:

We’re a young, ambitious, and small (but growing!) company that’s making a massive impact in ecommerce. 



Since 2015 we’ve been obsessed with building award-winning products and awesome brands that people fall in love with. We have 2 primary brands in our portfolio: 

(1) an international award-winning coffee products brand 
 / 
).

(2) an educational coaching program that helps entrepreneurs grow their businesses to create a positive impact in the world!



Our two recent flagship products became the world’s most successful launches in their categories. We’ve shipped more than 200,000 orders worldwide and we’re continuing to expand.



Our culture is transparent and candid. You’re always welcome (and encouraged!) to ask questions or express a difference of opinion. Learning and growth is a cornerstone of our culture, both personally and professionally. We work hard, and we have fun!







WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR:

We’re looking for a proactive and detail-oriented team player to fill this role—someone who thrives on solving problems, staying organized, and making things run smoothly across departments.



Do you love helping customers, supporting a team, and keeping operations organized? Do you get satisfaction from making systems work better and “getting things done”? If yes, we want you on our team!



You’re probably the type of person who:

• Enjoys supporting others and finding solutions to problems

• Has strong communication and organizational skills

• Likes working behind the scenes to make sure everything runs like clockwork

• Is excited to wear multiple hats and be part of a growing, fast-moving company

• Has worked directly with founders or in a startup, and enjoys that fast-paced, flexible vibe

We’re not just looking for someone who can support our team, we’re looking for someone to be proactive with taking ownership of tasks, solve problems independently, and grow into larger responsibilities over time.







YOUR OPPORTUNITY AND ROLE ON THE TEAM:

Our culture is collaborative, friendly, and open. We hire people who are smart, ambitious, and have a strong desire to learn and grow.



Sound like you? This is an exciting long-term opportunity!



This is a fully remote position. You’ll work directly with our founders and collaborate with our small but mighty team to support customer happiness, streamline operations, and take on projects that help the business grow. 



This is a foundational role — one where you’ll gain visibility into all parts of the company.



As you grow, your role will grow with you, based on your interests and strengths:

• Want to work improving our systems and processes? Awesome!

• Have ideas on how to improve our website? Fantastic!

• Want to learn how to manage projects? Let’s go!



We’re not just hiring for a role — we’re hiring for potential. 



Your responsibilities will span 4 key areas: customer experience (30-40%), operations/logistics (20-30%), executive support (10-20%), and special projects (10-20%) — giving you exposure across the business. These percentages are estimates and you can expect your day-to-day to shift and evolve as the business grows and as you take ownership. 



1. Customer Experience

• Provide
/chat-based support for our customers across our channels (Shopify, Amazon, and wholesale)

• Answer order and product questions, resolve issues promptly, and ensure customers feel heard and supported

• Support wholesale partners with order updates, invoicing, and coordination



2. Order Management & Platform Coordination

• Manage, monitor, and troubleshoot orders across Shopify, Amazon, and wholesale channels

• Troubleshoot and resolve shipping or fulfillment issues with partners and customers

• Maintain accurate product listings, inventory data, and logistics information across platforms



3. Executive & Admin Support

• Manage founder and team inboxes, schedule meetings, and organize internal documentation

• Assist with travel bookings, meeting preparation, and note-taking

• Support small projects by tracking timelines, managing tasks, and ensuring follow-through



4. Special Projects & Company Operations

• Conduct research projects to support decision-making (tools, systems, competitors)

• Support lead generation efforts through identifying new wholesale prospects, brand partners, or influencers

• Support planning and execution of events, expos, or marketing campaigns — manage timelines, logistics, and tasks

• Document and maintain SOPs for operational clarity and training

• Test and troubleshoot web pages, workflows, or customer journeys to ensure quality and functionality

• Take ownership of ad hoc “figure it out” tasks — we’ll guide you, but we value initiative and independent problem-solving mindset



You’ll be trained on all platforms and tools, and we’ll support your learning — but we’re looking for someone who’s excited to take initiative and build systems along the way. This role evolves — as you grow, so will your responsibilities.

title: Content Assistant
description:
Location : Remote

We are seeking a reliable, disciplined, and detail-oriented assistant to support our daily User-Generated Content (UGC) operations. This role is centered on ensuring consistency, accuracy, and quality in the management of creator-submitted content.



Key responsibilities include logging into the ARUGC creator platform each day to download newly submitted videos, as well as testing, reviewing, and organizing one video per day. The assistant will upload approved content to our internal platform, thoroughly review all associated metadata for completeness and accuracy, and submit finalized files with their corresponding metadata to the manager for final review and approval.



This position requires approximately one hour of work per day. While the workload is light, it demands a high level of consistency, strong attention to detail, and strict adherence to established processes. The role is well-suited for a self-motivated and organized individual who is comfortable working independently and managing recurring daily tasks while maintaining high standards of content quality.

title: Sales Closer (High-Ticket Marketing Services)
description:
Clientele Boosters is a fast-growing marketing & sales agency working with service-based businesses across Australia and internationally.



We are hiring Sales Closers to handle inbound and booked demos for marketing, SEO, paid ads, and sales enablement services.



This is a closer-only role. You will not be prospecting. You will be taking qualified demos, running structured sales calls, handling objections, and closing deals.



What you’ll be doing:



Taking 3–6 demos per day (ramping up over time)



Running 30–45 minute sales calls



Closing monthly retainer deals ($1,400+ p/m)



Same-day follow-ups and deal progression



Reviewing calls and improving conversion rate



Training & Support:



Structured onboarding



Shadowing senior closers



Assisted demos in your first month



Ongoing coaching and call reviews



This role is for you if:



You are confident on the phone



You enjoy persuasion, objection handling, and closing



You want performance-based upside



You can handle high deal flow without burning out



This role is NOT for you if:



You want a fixed salary with no pressure



You dislike targets or accountability



You are uncomfortable closing paid offers
